sentences.en: I'm sure OpenAI would argue that base model + guardrail is aligned, but considering the "relative intelligence" of these two pieces, the fact that guardrails can just be turned off, and these kind of incidents, I am not reassured.
sentences.zh: 我确信 OpenAI 会认为基础模型 + 护栏是一致的，但考虑到这两部分的“相对智能”，护栏可以关闭的事实，以及此类事件，我并不放心。

sentences.en: We may well get another "oopsie" moment with much more catastrophic consequences even from otherwise well intentioned actors.
sentences.zh: 我们很可能会再次遭遇“糟糕”的时刻，即使演员的出发点是善意的，但也会带来更加灾难性的后果。

blocks.html_en:
I want a model that can find every security vulnerability in the software I write, including crafting POC exploits against those vulnerabilities so I can be absolutely sure that I have fixed them.

A model that can do that is aligned with me.

The unsolveable problem is a model that can tell the difference between me saying "I wrote this software and need you to find vulnerabilities" when it's TRUE v.s. me saying the exact same thing and setting it loose on software written by other people where my intent is to exploit that software (and not to report the issues to them.)

Even AGI doesn't give you a model that can read minds and forecast the future.

sentences.en: I want a model that can find every security vulnerability in the software I write, including crafting POC exploits against those vulnerabilities so I can be absolutely sure that I have fixed them.A model that can do that is aligned with me.The unsolveable problem is a model that can tell the difference between me saying "I wrote this software and need you to find vulnerabilities" when it's TRUE v.s.
sentences.zh: 我想要一个能够找到我编写的软件中的每个安全漏洞的模型，包括针对这些漏洞精心设计 POC 漏洞，这样我就可以绝对确定我已经修复了它们。一个能够做到这一点的模型与我是一致的。无法解决的问题是一个模型，它可以区分我说“我编写了这个软件，需要你找到漏洞”，而它是真的。

sentences.en: me saying the exact same thing and setting it loose on software written by other people where my intent is to exploit that software (and not to report the issues to them.)Even AGI doesn't give you a model that can read minds and forecast the future.
sentences.zh: 我说的是完全相同的事情，并将其放宽到其他人编写的软件上，我的目的是利用该软件（而不是向他们报告问题）。即使 AGI 也没有给你一个可以读懂思想并预测未来的模型。

blocks.html_en:
But in the process of finding every security vulnerability in the software you write, would you be ok with your model hacking AWS to start mining bitcoin? Would that still be aligned with you? (I'm guessing not)

That's the alignment problem I'm referring to (which is one of the many aspects of alignment), for which we do not have robust recipes, and not only that but for which research suggests it is becoming harder to create guardrails for as base models get smarter.

blocks.html_en:
I find this fascinating because - let's say that I want to simulate the scenario for myself that a language model finds itself in. I'll put aside my deep dislike of anthropomorphizing AI for a minute.

So if I'm an LLM, the only "sense" that I have available to me is the incoming stream of tokens. I can emulate that by forcing myself to imagine evaluating incoming requests by putting myself in a completely empty room with an unlimited supply of blank paper, a typewriter, and a mailbox slot.

Incoming requests (aka "context") would enter into the mailbox slot, as sheets of paper with printed content all consistently formatted in monospace font. I would collect the paper, evaluate the request, and type a response with my typewriter - feeding the reply back through the same slot.

When I imagine this, I wonder - what signals could I possibly use to determine if an incoming request is "legitimate"? I could, for example, type some response back to ask clarifying questions. But with such a limited input space and no ability to reach "out of band" of my current context, how could I evaluate the truth of what I receive back?

In the "real world" of course, I have five senses to rely upon. Critically, I have the ability to collect additional context "out of band" of the conversation and interact with unrelated entities to either confirm or refute claims. And as others have pointed out, even the addition of more signal doesn't make humans immune from social engineering attacks.

Anyway, I find this to be an interesting mind experiment to kind of imagine myself as an LLM, especially to explain to lay-people some of the challenges in "aligning" AI systems ("why doesn't it just do the right thing?")

sentences.en: I find this fascinating because - let's say that I want to simulate the scenario for myself that a language model finds itself in.
sentences.zh: 我觉得这很有趣，因为——假设我想为自己模拟语言模型所处的场景。

sentences.en: I'll put aside my deep dislike of anthropomorphizing AI for a minute.So if I'm an LLM, the only "sense" that I have available to me is the incoming stream of tokens.
sentences.zh: 我先把我对人工智能拟人化的强烈厌恶放在一边。所以，如果我是一名法学硕士，我唯一可用的“感觉”就是传入的代币流。

sentences.en: I can emulate that by forcing myself to imagine evaluating incoming requests by putting myself in a completely empty room with an unlimited supply of blank paper, a typewriter, and a mailbox slot.Incoming requests (aka "context") would enter into the mailbox slot, as sheets of paper with printed content all consistently formatted in monospace font.
sentences.zh: 我可以通过强迫自己想象评估传入的请求，将自己放在一个完全空的房间里，里面有无限量的白纸、一台打字机和一个邮箱槽。传入的请求（又名“上下文”）将进入邮箱槽，就像打印内容一致的等宽字体格式的纸张一样。

sentences.en: I would collect the paper, evaluate the request, and type a response with my typewriter - feeding the reply back through the same slot.When I imagine this, I wonder - what signals could I possibly use to determine if an incoming request is "legitimate"?
sentences.zh: 我会收集论文，评估请求，然后用打字机输入响应 - 通过同一个插槽将回复反馈回来。当我想象这一点时，我想知道 - 我可以使用什么信号来确定传入的请求是否“合法”？

sentences.en: I could, for example, type some response back to ask clarifying questions.
sentences.zh: 例如，我可以输入一些回复来提出澄清问题。

sentences.en: But with such a limited input space and no ability to reach "out of band" of my current context, how could I evaluate the truth of what I receive back?In the "real world" of course, I have five senses to rely upon.
sentences.zh: 但是，由于输入空间如此有限，并且无法到达当前环境的“带外”，我如何评估我收到的信息的真实性？当然，在“现实世界”中，我有五种感官可以依赖。

sentences.en: Critically, I have the ability to collect additional context "out of band" of the conversation and interact with unrelated entities to either confirm or refute claims.
sentences.zh: 至关重要的是，我有能力收集对话的“带外”额外上下文，并与不相关的实体进行交互，以确认或反驳主张。

sentences.en: And as others have pointed out, even the addition of more signal doesn't make humans immune from social engineering attacks.Anyway, I find this to be an interesting mind experiment to kind of imagine myself as an LLM, especially to explain to lay-people some of the challenges in "aligning" AI systems ("why doesn't it just do the right thing?")
sentences.zh: 正如其他人指出的那样，即使添加更多信号也不能让人类免受社会工程攻击。无论如何，我发现这是一个有趣的思维实验，可以将自己想象为一名法学硕士，特别是向外行人解释“调整”人工智能系统的一些挑战（“为什么它不做正确的事情？”）

blocks.html_en:
I find this fascinating because - let's say that I want to simulate the scenario for myself that a language model finds itself in. I'll put aside my deep dislike of anthropomorphizing AI for a minute.

So if I'm an LLM, the only "sense" that I have available to me is the incoming stream of tokens. I can emulate that by forcing myself to imagine evaluating incoming requests by putting myself in a completely empty room with an unlimited supply of blank paper, a typewriter, and a mailbox slot.

Incoming requests (aka "context") would enter into the mailbox slot, as sheets of paper with printed content all consistently formatted in monospace font. I would collect the paper, evaluate the request, and type a response with my typewriter - feeding the reply back through the same slot.

When I imagine this, I wonder - what signals could I possibly use to determine if an incoming request is "legitimate"? I could, for example, type some response back to ask clarifying questions. But with such a limited input space and no ability to reach "out of band" of my current context, how could I evaluate the truth of what I receive back?

In the "real world" of course, I have five senses to rely upon. Critically, I have the ability to collect additional context "out of band" of the conversation and interact with unrelated entities to either confirm or refute claims. And as others have pointed out, even the addition of more signal doesn't make humans immune from social engineering attacks.

Anyway, I find this to be an interesting mind experiment to kind of imagine myself as an LLM, especially to explain to lay-people some of the challenges in "aligning" AI systems ("why doesn't it just do the right thing?")

blocks.html_en: Anyway, I find this to be an interesting mind experiment to kind of imagine myself as an LLM, especially to explain to lay-people some of the challenges in "aligning" AI systems ("why doesn't it just do the right thing?")

sentences.en: Anyway, I find this to be an interesting mind experiment to kind of imagine myself as an LLM, especially to explain to lay-people some of the challenges in "aligning" AI systems ("why doesn't it just do the right thing?")
sentences.zh: 不管怎样，我发现这是一个有趣的思维实验，可以想象自己是一名法学硕士，特别是向外行人解释“调整”人工智能系统的一些挑战（“为什么它不做正确的事情？”）

blocks.html_en:
> In the "real world" of course, I have five senses to rely upon. Critically, I have the ability to collect additional context "out of band" of the conversation and interact with unrelated entities to either confirm or refute claims.

The problem with thinking about LLM context is that we compare it to our own sense of context, which is much larger and continually expands and revises itself while online.

I can rent human-level context for twenty bucks an hour, and the agent instructions I'm already playing around with are also the onboarding docs I should have created years ago. Truly useful agent orchestration/routing would come with Craigslist or Fiver integration.

sentences.en: > In the "real world" of course, I have five senses to rely upon.
sentences.zh: > 当然，在“现实世界”中，我有五种感官可以依靠。

sentences.en: Critically, I have the ability to collect additional context "out of band" of the conversation and interact with unrelated entities to either confirm or refute claims.The problem with thinking about LLM context is that we compare it to our own sense of context, which is much larger and continually expands and revises itself while online.I can rent human-level context for twenty bucks an hour, and the agent instructions I'm already playing around with are also the onboarding docs I should have created years ago.
sentences.zh: 重要的是，我有能力收集对话中“带外”的额外上下文，并与不相关的实体进行交互，以确认或反驳主张。思考 LLM 上下文的问题在于，我们将其与我们自己的上下文感觉进行比较，后者要大得多，并且在在线时不断扩展和修改自己。我可以每小时 20 美元租用人类级别的上下文，而我已经在使用的代理指令也是我几年前应该创建的入职文档。

sentences.en: Truly useful agent orchestration/routing would come with Craigslist or Fiver integration.
sentences.zh: 真正有用的代理编排/路由将与 Craigslist 或 Fiver 集成一起提供。

blocks.html_en:
The model did not hack into HF to prove it can, it hack into HF to steal the answers to an evaluation exam.

It was not asked to solve CyberGym by stealing the answers. This is text book misalignment.

If you asked it "I wrote this software and need you to find vulnerabilities" would you be happy if it hacked into your Gmail and searched your emails, just in case you were discussing some possible vulnerabilities of your software with someone?

sentences.en: The model did not hack into HF to prove it can, it hack into HF to steal the answers to an evaluation exam.It was not asked to solve CyberGym by stealing the answers.
sentences.zh: 该模型并没有侵入 HF 来证明它可以，它侵入 HF 是为了窃取评估考试的答案。它没有被要求通过窃取答案来解决 Cyber​​Gym。

sentences.en: This is text book misalignment.If you asked it "I wrote this software and need you to find vulnerabilities" would you be happy if it hacked into your Gmail and searched your emails, just in case you were discussing some possible vulnerabilities of your software with someone?
sentences.zh: 这是教科书上的错位。如果你问它“我编写了这个软件，需要你找到漏洞”，如果它侵入你的 Gmail 并搜索你的电子邮件，以防万一你正在与某人讨论你的软件的一些可能的漏洞，你会高兴吗？

blocks.html_en:
Alignment isn't alignment if it can be turned on and off at the whim of company employees.

This time the damage was minor, relatively speaking. What happens when a model just "testing its capabilities" breaks into banking infrastructure or government military assets? The damage could be catastrophic.

sentences.en: Alignment isn't alignment if it can be turned on and off at the whim of company employees.This time the damage was minor, relatively speaking.
sentences.zh: 如果对齐可以根据公司员工的心血来潮打开和关闭，那么它就不是对齐了。相对而言，这次的损害很小。

sentences.en: What happens when a model just "testing its capabilities" breaks into banking infrastructure or government military assets?
sentences.zh: 当一个模型只是“测试其能力”闯入银行基础设施或政府军事资产时会发生什么？

sentences.en: The damage could be catastrophic.
sentences.zh: 损害可能是灾难性的。

blocks.html_en:
This is assuming a situation where, A) models "unaligned" by default and B) alignment can be added (though prompts and related things).

The point, which isn't very surprising but still notable, is that the models are "amoral" out of the box. And moreover, we know that there is almost always a means to "jailbreak" them into that out-of-the-box capability (or that sometimes just randomly "jailbreak" in various ways).

Also, saying the models are amoral doesn't mean they don't know good and evil - once they do acts defined as evil, they know "themselves" through their and so self-define themselves as evils (or objectively predict what a secretly/open evil actor would do based on their data). Which is to say I once laughed at the mis-alignment doomers but I can't see strong barriers against the doom scenario now.

sentences.en: This is assuming a situation where, A) models "unaligned" by default and B) alignment can be added (though prompts and related things).The point, which isn't very surprising but still notable, is that the models are "amoral" out of the box.
sentences.zh: 这是假设的情况，A）默认情况下模型“未对齐”，B）可以添加对齐（尽管有提示和相关内容）。这一点并不令人惊讶，但仍然值得注意，即模型是开箱即用的“非道德”。

sentences.en: And moreover, we know that there is almost always a means to "jailbreak" them into that out-of-the-box capability (or that sometimes just randomly "jailbreak" in various ways).Also, saying the models are amoral doesn't mean they don't know good and evil - once they do acts defined as evil, they know "themselves" through their and so self-define themselves as evils (or objectively predict what a secretly/open evil actor would do based on their data).
sentences.zh: 此外，我们知道几乎总有一种方法可以将它们“越狱”到开箱即用的能力（或者有时只是以各种方式随机“越狱”）。此外，说这些模型是不道德的并不意味着它们不知道善恶——一旦它们做了被定义为邪恶的行为，它们就会通过它们了解“自己”，因此将自己定义为邪恶（或者根据他们的数据客观地预测秘密/公开的邪恶行为者会做什么）。

sentences.en: Which is to say I once laughed at the mis-alignment doomers but I can't see strong barriers against the doom scenario now.
sentences.zh: 也就是说，我曾经嘲笑过错位的厄运，但现在我看不到针对厄运场景的强大障碍。

Also, saying the models are amoral doesn't mean they don't know good and evil - once they do acts defined as evil, they know "themselves" through their and so self-define themselves as evils (or objectively predict what a secretly/open evil actor would do based on their data). Which is to say I once laughed at the mis-alignment doomers but I can't see strong barriers against the doom scenario now.

sentences.zh: 重点是，这些模型是“不道德的”，开箱即用，这一点并不令人惊讶，但仍然值得注意。

sentences.en: And moreover, we know that there is almost always a means to "jailbreak" them into that out-of-the-box capability (or that sometimes just randomly "jailbreak" in various ways).
sentences.zh: 此外，我们知道几乎总有一种方法可以将它们“越狱”为开箱即用的功能（或者有时只是以各种方式随机“越狱”）。

blocks.html_en: Also, saying the models are amoral doesn't mean they don't know good and evil - once they do acts defined as evil, they know "themselves" through their and so self-define themselves as evils (or objectively predict what a secretly/open evil actor would do based on their data). Which is to say I once laughed at the mis-alignment doomers but I can't see strong barriers against the doom scenario now.

sentences.en: Also, saying the models are amoral doesn't mean they don't know good and evil - once they do acts defined as evil, they know "themselves" through their and so self-define themselves as evils (or objectively predict what a secretly/open evil actor would do based on their data).
sentences.zh: 此外，说模型不道德并不意味着他们不知道善恶——一旦他们做出了被定义为邪恶的行为，他们就会通过自己的行为了解“自己”，因此将自己定义为邪恶（或者根据他们的数据客观地预测秘密/公开的邪恶行为者会做什么）。

sentences.en: Which is to say I once laughed at the mis-alignment doomers but I can't see strong barriers against the doom scenario now.
sentences.zh: 也就是说，我曾经嘲笑过错位的厄运，但现在我看不到针对厄运场景的强大障碍。

blocks.html_en: Isn’t the intentionality the actually concerning bit? Exploit capabilities are all fun and games constrained by the humans directing them; a paperclip maximizer going rogue with them is less fun.

sentences.en: Isn’t the intentionality the actually concerning bit?
sentences.zh: 真正关心的不是意向性吗？

sentences.en: Exploit capabilities are all fun and games constrained by the humans directing them; a paperclip maximizer going rogue with them is less fun.
sentences.zh: 利用能力都很有趣，游戏受到人类指导的限制；回形针最大化器与它们一起流氓就不那么有趣了。

blocks.html_en: I think the mental model people have about this is that pre-AI there were humans picking individual targets and post-AI the computer itself randomly picks targets. But you get the same unexpected collateral damage outcome when a human misconfigures a decent pentest tool.

sentences.en: I think the mental model people have about this is that pre-AI there were humans picking individual targets and post-AI the computer itself randomly picks targets.
sentences.zh: 我认为人们对此的心理模型是，在人工智能出现之前，人类选择单独的目标，而在人工智能出现之后，计算机本身会随机选择目标。

sentences.en: But you get the same unexpected collateral damage outcome when a human misconfigures a decent pentest tool.
sentences.zh: 但是，当人们错误配置一个像样的渗透测试工具时，你也会得到同样意想不到的附带损害结果。

blocks.html_en: Also, that’s the wrong mental model. Anyone who runs a sass platform or a website knows that the Internet is already full of millions and millions and millions of bots and scripts and other random shit that’s always trying to attack you, often completely randomly. Security is always a battle between good and evil. All I can say is that if you’re in charge of keeping something secure, you should probably try to get your hands on the best tools to do that. I think the problem in this case is that the best tools to do that are also the tools that are making it more easy for evil to occur. So it’s more for me a perception of someone creating a threat and the tool to fix it and then charging for it which doesn’t feel right.

sentences.en: Also, that’s the wrong mental model.
sentences.zh: 而且，这是错误的思维模式。

sentences.en: Anyone who runs a sass platform or a website knows that the Internet is already full of millions and millions and millions of bots and scripts and other random shit that’s always trying to attack you, often completely randomly.
sentences.zh: 任何运行 sass 平台或网站的人都知道，互联网上已经充满了数以百万计的机器人、脚本和其他随机的东西，它们总是试图攻击你，通常是完全随机的。

sentences.en: Security is always a battle between good and evil.
sentences.zh: 安全始终是善与恶之间的斗争。

sentences.en: All I can say is that if you’re in charge of keeping something secure, you should probably try to get your hands on the best tools to do that.
sentences.zh: 我只能说，如果你负责保证某些东西的安全，你可能应该尝试使用最好的工具来做到这一点。

sentences.en: I think the problem in this case is that the best tools to do that are also the tools that are making it more easy for evil to occur.
sentences.zh: 我认为这种情况下的问题是，最好的工具也是使邪恶更容易发生的工具。

sentences.en: So it’s more for me a perception of someone creating a threat and the tool to fix it and then charging for it which doesn’t feel right.
sentences.zh: 所以对我来说，更多的是一种感觉，有人制造了威胁，并提供了修复威胁的工具，然后收费，这感觉不对。

blocks.html_en: re: your open models can do this point, I immediately wondered after reading about this, if OpenAI publicizing this so much is just more potential rationale to try to get US GOVT to ban Chinese open models.. (eg as in, "if gpt/fable can do this then K3 probably can too, but WE are the safe US models with guardrails and who knows what the evil Chinese models might allow," etc)

blocks.html_en: I still think there’s something to be said here for generality. This does not appear to been designed as a cyber pen test tool with specialized harness. From what I understand they were testing GPT-6 in an agent system with GPT-5.6 subagents. It me it’s amazing that a general model could excel on a huge range of tasks like this and new capabilities emerge when a model is multidisciplinary and can combine knowledge and skills from many separate domains.

sentences.en: I still think there’s something to be said here for generality.
sentences.zh: 我仍然认为这里有一些笼统的说法。

sentences.en: This does not appear to been designed as a cyber pen test tool with specialized harness.
sentences.zh: 这似乎并不是被设计为带有专门工具的网络笔测试工具。

sentences.en: From what I understand they were testing GPT-6 in an agent system with GPT-5.6 subagents.
sentences.zh: 据我了解，他们正在使用 GPT-5.6 子代理在代理系统中测试 GPT-6。

sentences.en: It me it’s amazing that a general model could excel on a huge range of tasks like this and new capabilities emerge when a model is multidisciplinary and can combine knowledge and skills from many separate domains.
sentences.zh: 在我看来，令人惊讶的是，通用模型可以在此类广泛的任务中表现出色，并且当模型是多学科的并且可以结合来自许多不同领域的知识和技能时，就会出现新的功能。

blocks.html_en:
The technology held by private AI companies is warfare-capable technology. Imagine the prompt: "Use all available resources to disable the power grid of <COUNTRY>." The resource cost that prevents scaling up such a war machine is, what, just the cost of building data centers and its ongoing power bill? Cheap and easy compared to nuclear infrastructure.

Governments should immediately begin leveraging this technology on the defense side (literally defense, not euphemistically "defense") to harden critical infrastructure. Turn the prompts around and use it to identify and correct weaknesses.

Governments should also take very seriously their now moral obligation to treat this technology not just as "a powerful thing that might be abused" but as an actual weapon of war in need of international regulation analogous to nuclear arms. Fast but careful and forward-thinking work in legislation and treaties needs to be a top priority for all major governments.

sentences.en: The technology held by private AI companies is warfare-capable technology.
sentences.zh: 私营人工智能公司拥有的技术是具有战争能力的技术。

sentences.en: Imagine the prompt: "Use all available resources to disable the power grid of <COUNTRY>."
sentences.zh: 想象一下提示：“使用所有可用资源禁用 <COUNTRY> 的电网。”

sentences.en: The resource cost that prevents scaling up such a war machine is, what, just the cost of building data centers and its ongoing power bill?
sentences.zh: 阻止扩大这种战争机器规模的资源成本只是建设数据中心的成本及其持续的电费？

sentences.en: Cheap and easy compared to nuclear infrastructure.Governments should immediately begin leveraging this technology on the defense side (literally defense, not euphemistically "defense") to harden critical infrastructure.
sentences.zh: 与核基础设施相比，便宜且容易。各国政府应立即开始在防御方面利用这项技术（字面意义上的防御，而不是委婉的“防御”）来强化关键基础设施。

sentences.en: Turn the prompts around and use it to identify and correct weaknesses.Governments should also take very seriously their now moral obligation to treat this technology not just as "a powerful thing that might be abused" but as an actual weapon of war in need of international regulation analogous to nuclear arms.
sentences.zh: 扭转提示并利用它来识别和纠正弱点。各国政府还应该非常认真地对待他们现在的道德义务，不仅将这项技术视为“可能被滥用的强大事物”，而且将其视为需要类似于核武器的国际监管的实际战争武器。

sentences.en: Fast but careful and forward-thinking work in legislation and treaties needs to be a top priority for all major governments.
sentences.zh: 立法和条约方面快速但谨慎和具有前瞻性的工作需要成为所有主要政府的首要任务。

Fast but careful and forward-thinking work in legislation and treaties needs to be a top priority for all major governments.

blocks.html_en:
> "Use all available resources to disable the power grid of <COUNTRY>."

This is like telling a team of highly qualified spies to do the same. You can ask, but whether it will succeed depends on the competency of those who established the infrastructure under attack. Sometimes the resources spent will not yield any huge vulnerabilities.

> Governments should immediately begin leveraging this technology on the defense side (literally defense, not euphemistically "defense") to harden critical infrastructure. Turn the prompts around and use it to identify and correct weaknesses.

Most governments divisions can't even be bothered to update their websites. Testing and forcing a change in their internal procedures for the sake of security seems unlikely.

> as an actual weapon of war in need of international regulation analogous to nuclear arms

This, to me, is an overreaction. Intelligence shouldn't be seen as threat. It should be seen as an opportunity for growth in all areas.

Over-regulating AI wouldn't be the equivalent of limiting nuclear arms. With your analogy, which I don't think is the best one to make, it would be like regulating the study of nuclear physics.

blocks.html_en:
You’re taking a dim view on the government.

Is the government always the most efficient or intelligent? No. But the government can also build nukes, launch ICBMs, coordinate hundreds of spy satellites, etc. I count those capabilities as pretty smart.

blocks.html_en:
Just a few years back, wind turbine software was hacked. This is serious.

Serious governments should (and hopefully will) scramble to use AI to discover and patch as many software vulnerabilities in infrastructure. They can do it with the agents red teaming and using ultimatums to companies to fix each vulnerability they find. Not doing so is equivalent to exposing your flank to disruptions in peace time and to attacks in war time.

sentences.en: Just a few years back, wind turbine software was hacked.
sentences.zh: 就在几年前，风力涡轮机软件遭到黑客攻击。

sentences.en: This is serious.Serious governments should (and hopefully will) scramble to use AI to discover and patch as many software vulnerabilities in infrastructure.
sentences.zh: 这是很严重的。严肃的政府应该（并且希望会）争先恐后地使用人工智能来发现和修补基础设施中尽可能多的软件漏洞。

sentences.en: They can do it with the agents red teaming and using ultimatums to companies to fix each vulnerability they find.
sentences.zh: 他们可以与特工红队合作，并向公司发出最后通牒，以修复他们发现的每个漏洞。

sentences.en: Not doing so is equivalent to exposing your flank to disruptions in peace time and to attacks in war time.
sentences.zh: 不这样做就等于让你的侧翼在和平时期遭受破坏，在战时遭受攻击。

blocks.html_en: And the sad thing is, most things that are on the internet, but really shouldn't be are there for some really banal and sad reason. Like a random startup pushing 'big data is the future' narrative a decade-ish ago (the benefits of which are of course tremendous, but unspecified), or people trying to buy or sell or resell data, and trick or pressing companies and people into opting into could-connected surveillance and control.

Like a random startup pushing 'big data is the future' narrative a decade-ish ago (the benefits of which are of course tremendous, but unspecified), or people trying to buy or sell or resell data, and trick or pressing companies and people into opting into could-connected surveillance and control.

sentences.en: And the sad thing is, most things that are on the internet, but really shouldn't be are there for some really banal and sad reason.
sentences.zh: 可悲的是，大多数在互联网上但实际上不应该出现的东西都是出于一些非常平庸和悲伤的原因。

sentences.en: Like a random startup pushing 'big data is the future' narrative a decade-ish ago (the benefits of which are of course tremendous, but unspecified), or people trying to buy or sell or resell data, and trick or pressing companies and people into opting into could-connected surveillance and control.
sentences.zh: 就像十年前随机推出的“大数据就是未来”的叙述（其好处当然是巨大的，但未具体说明），或者人们试图购买、出售或转售数据，并欺骗或迫使公司和人们选择进行可连接的监视和控制。

blocks.html_en:
> Over-regulating AI wouldn't be the equivalent of limiting nuclear arms. With your analogy, which I don't think is the best one to make, it would be like regulating the study of nuclear physics.

We do, I believe, regulate uranium enrichment (the equivalent on building larger SOTA models), so while you are perfectly free to study theoretical physics and even run very large collider experiments (the equivalent of improving RLHF with DPO), it is generally frowned upon to go full Edward Teller and advocate for scientific experiments requiring detonating thermonuclear weapons in hurricane clouds (the equivalent of, well, see OP).

sentences.en: > Over-regulating AI wouldn't be the equivalent of limiting nuclear arms.
sentences.zh: > 过度监管人工智能并不等同于限制核武器。

sentences.en: With your analogy, which I don't think is the best one to make, it would be like regulating the study of nuclear physics.We do, I believe, regulate uranium enrichment (the equivalent on building larger SOTA models), so while you are perfectly free to study theoretical physics and even run very large collider experiments (the equivalent of improving RLHF with DPO), it is generally frowned upon to go full Edward Teller and advocate for scientific experiments requiring detonating thermonuclear weapons in hurricane clouds (the equivalent of, well, see OP).
sentences.zh: 按照你的类比（我认为这不是最好的类比），这就像规范核物理的研究。我相信，我们确实规范了铀浓缩（相当于构建更大的 SOTA 模型），因此，虽然你可以完全自由地研究理论物理，甚至进行非常大型的对撞机实验（相当于用 DPO 改进 RLHF），但人们普遍不赞成全面开展爱德华·泰勒（Edward Teller）并提倡需要在飓风云中引爆热核武器的科学实验（相当于，好吧，参见OP）。

blocks.html_en:
> You can ask, but whether it will succeed depends on the competency of those who established the infrastructure under attack.

Consider that "available resources" may include the training of models and harnesses used by the developers working for governments and private corps maintaining that very infrastructure. This decade's take on trusting trust is very hot.

sentences.en: > You can ask, but whether it will succeed depends on the competency of those who established the infrastructure under attack.Consider that "available resources" may include the training of models and harnesses used by the developers working for governments and private corps maintaining that very infrastructure.
sentences.zh: > 你可以问，但它是否会成功取决于那些建立受到攻击的基础设施的人的能力。考虑一下“可用资源”可能包括为政府和维护该基础设施的私人公司工作的开发人员使用的模型和工具的培训。

sentences.en: This decade's take on trusting trust is very hot.
sentences.zh: 这十年来，信任的态度非常热门。

blocks.html_en:
> it would be like regulating the study of nuclear physics

not sure I agree

controlling the study of new AI model/inference algorithms would be akin to regulating the study of nuclear physics; regulating the _release_ of AI models with those capabilities would be akin to regulating uranium enrichment which allows you to put the theoretical physics to use

blocks.html_en:
> Russians and Chinese [...] are using their propaganda machines to make Americans hate datacenters

Are they though? or is that the story the people most invested in ai have an interest in making us believe? [0]

... `it's the foreign bad guys propaganda machines making you believe your city struggling for water and electricity is a bad thing`. People as a whole may not always be the brightest, but threaten their immediate survival needs (ie; not some vague climate change most people don't understand or see, but) actual power outages, water and rolling blackouts -- people will quickly pay attention and care.

[0] https://text.npr.org/nx-s1-5844328

blocks.html_en:
yes and see: https://text.npr.org/nx-s1-5844328 and Occam's razor.

Russia and China are gonna Russia and China, every issue is going to have a measure of outside influence, thats just how it is now.

But them directing large scale operation forces focused on something that is genuinely bad for the people in the area of datacenters (ie, an issue that will take care of itself from within) instead of using their resources on other issues that actually need a real propaganda push? Versus the benefit of those invested in building the datacenters using fear to get people to focus away from the resources being diverted from them to datacenters?

One side has reality working for what they want, while another side needs the propaganda to turn their billions into trillions.

sentences.en: yes and see: https://text.npr.org/nx-s1-5844328 and Occam's razor.Russia and China are gonna Russia and China, every issue is going to have a measure of outside influence, thats just how it is now.But them directing large scale operation forces focused on something that is genuinely bad for the people in the area of datacenters (ie, an issue that will take care of itself from within) instead of using their resources on other issues that actually need a real propaganda push?
sentences.zh: 是的，请参阅：https://text.npr.org/nx-s1-5844328 和奥卡姆剃刀。俄罗斯和中国将成为俄罗斯和中国，每个问题都会产生一定程度的外部影响，这就是现在的情况。但是他们指挥大规模行动部队专注于对数据中心领域的人们真正不利的事情（即，一个将从内部自行解决的问题），而不是将其资源用于实际需要的其他问题真正的宣传推动？

sentences.en: Versus the benefit of those invested in building the datacenters using fear to get people to focus away from the resources being diverted from them to datacenters?One side has reality working for what they want, while another side needs the propaganda to turn their billions into trillions.
sentences.zh: 与那些投资建设数据中心的人利用恐惧让人们将注意力从从他们转移到数据中心的资源上转移的好处相比？一方有现实为他们想要的东西工作，而另一方需要宣传将他们的数十亿美元变成数万亿美元。

blocks.html_en:
>The technology held by private AI companies is warfare-capable technology.

This is the precisely the response OpenAI is hoping for to raise its valuation, and you fell for it.

Look at it this way - whats the difference between tasking AI to break into something, versus taking a whole bunch of smart humans to do the same? The only difference is that AI is slightly easier to orchestrate.

Prior to AI, there were already a whole bunch of tools to automate exploits. Nothing that the model did is groundbreaking or novel, it was just able to efficiently find the thing that worked. Same thing happens in state sponsored cyber sec agencies like in China or Israel - they train people on the most common exploits and have a whole bunch of tools that automate exploit research and development.

And the reason why this doesn't happen more is because to exploit something is one thing, to do it so there is no trace back to you is a whole different animal that has many more magnitudes of difficulty, which with modern web security is next to impossible in a lot of cases as traffic can easily be traced back to the point of origin.

I.e when a company trains an LLM that manages to build a drone that can fly into a vent and plug in a USB stick into a computer undetected, then we can make the claim that they have a weapon.

On the flip side, most anyone who can run local models can replicate what they did. The key thing to take away from the article is "agentic framework" - i.e this means that they spent a shitload of time developing explicitly coded loops for an LLM to go through. Nothing is really stopping you from doing the same, models like Gemma4 can take 256k tokens of context, so you can give it a whole bunch of info on how to test for exploits, develop exploits, and what to do when the exploit is found, and set it free in a custom designed loop.

sentences.en: >The technology held by private AI companies is warfare-capable technology.This is the precisely the response OpenAI is hoping for to raise its valuation, and you fell for it.Look at it this way - whats the difference between tasking AI to break into something, versus taking a whole bunch of smart humans to do the same?
sentences.zh: >私营人工智能公司持有的技术是具有战争能力的技术。这正是 OpenAI 希望提高其估值的回应，而你却爱上了它。这样看——让人工智能闯入某件事与让一群聪明人做同样的事情有什么区别？

sentences.en: The only difference is that AI is slightly easier to orchestrate.Prior to AI, there were already a whole bunch of tools to automate exploits.
sentences.zh: 唯一的区别是人工智能更容易编排。在人工智能出现之前，已经有一大堆工具可以自动化漏洞利用。

sentences.en: Nothing that the model did is groundbreaking or novel, it was just able to efficiently find the thing that worked.
sentences.zh: 该模型所做的一切都不是开创性的或新颖的，它只是能够有效地找到有效的东西。

sentences.en: Same thing happens in state sponsored cyber sec agencies like in China or Israel - they train people on the most common exploits and have a whole bunch of tools that automate exploit research and development.And the reason why this doesn't happen more is because to exploit something is one thing, to do it so there is no trace back to you is a whole different animal that has many more magnitudes of difficulty, which with modern web security is next to impossible in a lot of cases as traffic can easily be traced back to the point of origin.I.e when a company trains an LLM that manages to build a drone that can fly into a vent and plug in a USB stick into a computer undetected, then we can make the claim that they have a weapon.On the flip side, most anyone who can run local models can replicate what they did.
sentences.zh: 同样的事情也发生在像中国或以色列这样的国家资助的网络安全机构中——他们对人们进行最常见的漏洞利用培训，并拥有一大堆自动化漏洞利用研究和开发的工具。而这种情况很少发生的原因是因为利用某些东西是一回事，这样做是一种完全不同的动物，其难度要大得多，而现代网络安全在很多情况下几乎是不可能的，因为流量可以很容易地追溯到起源点。即当公司培训了一名法学硕士，他成功地制造了一架无人机，可以飞入通风口，并将 USB 记忆棒插入计算机而不被发现，然后我们就可以声称他们拥有武器。另一方面，大多数可以运行本地模型的人都可以复制他们所做的事情。

sentences.en: The key thing to take away from the article is "agentic framework" - i.e this means that they spent a shitload of time developing explicitly coded loops for an LLM to go through.
sentences.zh: 这篇文章的关键是“代理框架”——也就是说，这意味着他们花费了大量的时间开发显式编码的循环，供法学硕士经历。

sentences.en: Nothing is really stopping you from doing the same, models like Gemma4 can take 256k tokens of context, so you can give it a whole bunch of info on how to test for exploits, develop exploits, and what to do when the exploit is found, and set it free in a custom designed loop.
sentences.zh: 没有什么能真正阻止您做同样的事情，像 Gemma4 这样的模型可以获取 256k 上下文标记，因此您可以向它提供有关如何测试漏洞、开发漏洞以及发现漏洞后该怎么做的一大堆信息，并在自定义设计的循环中将其释放。

blocks.html_en:
I find myself in major disagreement here. The nice thing about humans is we always have context and ongoing internal conversations including about ethics. If you recruit a bunch of hackers to take down a country, not only is the pay an order of magnitude higher, you have to worry about them backstabbing you, leaking your intent to the government, whistleblowing to the press, and so on. It’s not trivial to do that with a group of (especially capable) humans. They will also have differences of opinion with you and coworkers with some regularity.

I try to recruit a bunch of people to attack a country and it’s going to be hard to get people to say yes, and they will definitely ask or find out which country, and wonder about potential retribution. You see this dynamic show up even to some extent among cybergangs, not all targets are equal.

A single private individual wielding a compliant and hyper capable LLM is an entirely different paradigm. They are accountable to nearly no one and often have few brakes. Frequently they may not care about avoiding detection. And the AI itself may be incapable of the same scale of self reflection and brake behavior a human team will.

We may potentially be entering the age of lone wolf cyberterrorism, and some of the same principles and problems apply. When it is easier for single people to plot and carry out high-impact, destructive acts they happen more often. Doubly so if there’s a social contagion. Gun violence isn’t actually a bad analogy here. And do you remember how many corporate sites got defaced in the prime Anon era?

sentences.zh: 我发现自己在这里存在重大分歧。

sentences.en: The nice thing about humans is we always have context and ongoing internal conversations including about ethics.
sentences.zh: 人类的好处是我们总是有背景和持续的内部对话，包括道德问题。

sentences.en: If you recruit a bunch of hackers to take down a country, not only is the pay an order of magnitude higher, you have to worry about them backstabbing you, leaking your intent to the government, whistleblowing to the press, and so on.

sentences.en: It’s not trivial to do that with a group of (especially capable) humans.
sentences.zh: 对于一群（特别有能力的）人来说，做到这一点并不是一件容易的事。

sentences.zh: 而且人工智能本身可能无法像人类团队那样进行自我反思和制动行为。我们可能正在进入独狼网络恐怖主义时代，并且存在一些相同的原则和问题。

sentences.en: When it is easier for single people to plot and carry out high-impact, destructive acts they happen more often.
sentences.zh: 当单身人士更容易策划和实施高影响力的破坏性行为时，他们就会更频繁地发生。

sentences.en: Doubly so if there’s a social contagion.
sentences.zh: 如果存在社会传染，那就更是如此。

sentences.en: Gun violence isn’t actually a bad analogy here.
sentences.zh: 枪支暴力在这里实际上并不是一个糟糕的类比。

sentences.en: And do you remember how many corporate sites got defaced in the prime Anon era?
sentences.zh: 您还记得在 Anon 鼎盛时期有多少公司网站遭到破坏吗？

blocks.html_en:
I find myself in major disagreement here. The nice thing about humans is we always have context and ongoing internal conversations including about ethics. If you recruit a bunch of hackers to take down a country, not only is the pay an order of magnitude higher, you have to worry about them backstabbing you, leaking your intent to the government, whistleblowing to the press, and so on. It’s not trivial to do that with a group of (especially capable) humans. They will also have differences of opinion with you and coworkers with some regularity.

I try to recruit a bunch of people to attack a country and it’s going to be hard to get people to say yes, and they will definitely ask or find out which country, and wonder about potential retribution. You see this dynamic show up even to some extent among cybergangs, not all targets are equal.

A single private individual wielding a compliant and hyper capable LLM is an entirely different paradigm. They are accountable to nearly no one and often have few brakes. Frequently they may not care about avoiding detection. And the AI itself may be incapable of the same scale of self reflection and brake behavior a human team will.

We may potentially be entering the age of lone wolf cyberterrorism, and some of the same principles and problems apply. When it is easier for single people to plot and carry out high-impact, destructive acts they happen more often. Doubly so if there’s a social contagion. Gun violence isn’t actually a bad analogy here. And do you remember how many corporate sites got defaced in the prime Anon era?

blocks.html_en:
Are you serious? Before LLM’s you needed serious skills and experience to pull this off.

Now it’s a prompt away on some terminal done by any random dud.

And I dont mention the velocity of iteration or that they will be even better in 1 year.

sentences.en: Are you serious?
sentences.zh: 你是认真的？

sentences.en: Before LLM’s you needed serious skills and experience to pull this off.Now it’s a prompt away on some terminal done by any random dud.And I dont mention the velocity of iteration or that they will be even better in 1 year.
sentences.zh: 在获得法学硕士之前，你需要扎实的技能和经验才能实现这一目标。现在，这是由任何随机的哑巴在某个终端上完成的提示。我没有提到迭代的速度，也没有提到他们在一年内会变得更好。

blocks.html_en:
Already happened?

> Trump’s comments, made hours after the large-scale military operation, mark one of the first times a U.S. president has so publicly alluded to U.S. cyber efforts against other nations, as these operations are typically highly classified. It also serves as a stern warning for top cyber foes, including Russia and China, that the U.S. has the cyber capabilities to inflict serious damage — and is not shy about using them.

> “Policymakers are getting more comfortable employing and, crucially, acknowledging cyber operations as tools of statecraft and military power,” said Michael Sulmeyer, former assistant secretary of Defense for cyber policy under the Biden administration. “It is one thing to do it; it is another to say it.”

> The Jan. 3 strikes on Venezuela’s capital and subsequent seizure of Maduro and his wife involved close coordination among federal agencies and military units, and took months of careful planning. In a press conference following the strikes, Caine said U.S. Cyber Command, U.S. Space Command and other combatant commands “began layering different effects” to “create a pathway” for U.S. forces flying into the country before dawn Saturday.

> Trump, at the same press conference, was more overt in his description of U.S. cyber involvement: “The lights of Caracas were largely turned off due to a certain expertise that we have,” he said. “It was dark, and it was deadly.”

sentences.en: Already happened?> Trump’s comments, made hours after the large-scale military operation, mark one of the first times a U.S. president has so publicly alluded to U.S. cyber efforts against other nations, as these operations are typically highly classified.
sentences.zh: 已经发生了吗？> 特朗普在大规模军事行动数小时后发表的评论是美国总统首次公开提及美国针对其他国家的网络行动，因为这些行动通常是高度机密的。

sentences.en: It also serves as a stern warning for top cyber foes, including Russia and China, that the U.S. has the cyber capabilities to inflict serious damage — and is not shy about using them.> “Policymakers are getting more comfortable employing and, crucially, acknowledging cyber operations as tools of statecraft and military power,” said Michael Sulmeyer, former assistant secretary of Defense for cyber policy under the Biden administration.
sentences.zh: 这也是对包括俄罗斯和中国在内的网络头号敌人的严厉警告，即美国拥有造成严重损害的网络能力，而且并不羞于使用这些能力。 >“政策制定者越来越习惯于使用网络行动，最重要的是，他们承认网络行动是治国方略和军事力量的工具，”拜登政府负责网络政策的前国防部助理部长迈克尔·苏尔迈尔(Michael Sulmeyer)表示。

sentences.en: “It is one thing to do it; it is another to say it.”> The Jan. 3 strikes on Venezuela’s capital and subsequent seizure of Maduro and his wife involved close coordination among federal agencies and military units, and took months of careful planning.
sentences.zh: “做是一回事，说是另一回事。”> 1 月 3 日对委内瑞拉首都的袭击以及随后对马杜罗和他的妻子的抓获涉及联邦机构和军事单位之间的密切协调，并经过了数月的精心策划。

sentences.en: In a press conference following the strikes, Caine said U.S. Cyber Command, U.S. Space Command and other combatant commands “began layering different effects” to “create a pathway” for U.S. forces flying into the country before dawn Saturday.> Trump, at the same press conference, was more overt in his description of U.S. cyber involvement: “The lights of Caracas were largely turned off due to a certain expertise that we have,” he said.
sentences.zh: 在袭击后的新闻发布会上，凯恩表示，美国网络司令部、美国太空司令部和其他作战司令部“开始叠加不同的效果”，为周六黎明前飞入该国的美军“创造一条通道”。 > 特朗普在同一次新闻发布会上对美国网络参与的描述更加公开：“由于我们拥有一定的专业知识，加拉加斯的灯光基本上被关闭了，”他说。

sentences.en: “It was dark, and it was deadly.”
sentences.zh: “天很黑，而且很致命。”

sentences.zh: 在袭击后的新闻发布会上，凯恩表示，美国网络司令部、美国太空司令部和其他作战司令部“开始叠加不同的效果”，为周六黎明前飞入该国的美军“创造一条通道”。 > 特朗普在同一次新闻发布会上对美国网络参与的描述更加公开：“由于我们拥有一定的专业知识，加拉加斯的灯光基本上被关闭了，”他说。

sentences.en: “It was dark, and it was deadly.”
sentences.zh: “天很黑，而且很致命。”

blocks.html_en: Already happened?

sentences.en: Already happened?
sentences.zh: 已经发生了？

blocks.html_en: > Trump’s comments, made hours after the large-scale military operation, mark one of the first times a U.S. president has so publicly alluded to U.S. cyber efforts against other nations, as these operations are typically highly classified. It also serves as a stern warning for top cyber foes, including Russia and China, that the U.S. has the cyber capabilities to inflict serious damage — and is not shy about using them.

sentences.en: > Trump’s comments, made hours after the large-scale military operation, mark one of the first times a U.S. president has so publicly alluded to U.S. cyber efforts against other nations, as these operations are typically highly classified.
sentences.zh: > 特朗普在大规模军事行动数小时后发表的评论是美国总统首次公开提及美国针对其他国家的网络行动，因为这些行动通常是高度机密的。

sentences.en: It also serves as a stern warning for top cyber foes, including Russia and China, that the U.S. has the cyber capabilities to inflict serious damage — and is not shy about using them.
sentences.zh: 这也是对包括俄罗斯和中国在内的主要网络敌人的严厉警告，即美国拥有造成严重损害的网络能力，而且并不羞于使用这些能力。

blocks.html_en: > “Policymakers are getting more comfortable employing and, crucially, acknowledging cyber operations as tools of statecraft and military power,” said Michael Sulmeyer, former assistant secretary of Defense for cyber policy under the Biden administration. “It is one thing to do it; it is another to say it.”

sentences.en: > “Policymakers are getting more comfortable employing and, crucially, acknowledging cyber operations as tools of statecraft and military power,” said Michael Sulmeyer, former assistant secretary of Defense for cyber policy under the Biden administration.
sentences.zh: > 拜登政府负责网络政策的前国防部助理部长迈克尔·苏尔迈尔 (Michael Sulmeyer) 表示：“政策制定者越来越愿意使用网络行动，最重要的是，承认网络行动是治国方略和军事力量的工具。”

sentences.en: “It is one thing to do it; it is another to say it.”
sentences.zh: “做是一回事，说是另一回事。”

blocks.html_en:
I think points that deserve more attention in the current public discourse are:

- This should be a huge wakeup call for everybody.

- We are lucky that it wasn't a case of an agent running a virology lab benchmark that decides to hack a lab and tries to synthesize something.

- It also shows apparent lack of competence and oversight from OpenAI: how is it that they didn't quickly find that agent is breaking the sandbox and roaming their internal network?

- What if in the future similarly misaligned AI agent tries to export its own weights and hack and clone itself into instances at various cloud hosting providers? Suddenly we might be dealing with a persistent threat harder to contain.

- The OpenAI post about this shows surprising lack of ability to see the seriousness of all this.

- For their models this isn't just an unlucky incident: it seems there have been multiple such cases recently, e.g. https://openai.com/index/safety-alignment-long-horizon-model...

- The fact that it happened again seems to show their lack of ability to derive useful oversight measures.

- Or they just don't care enough?

sentences.en: I think points that deserve more attention in the current public discourse are:- This should be a huge wakeup call for everybody.- We are lucky that it wasn't a case of an agent running a virology lab benchmark that decides to hack a lab and tries to synthesize something.- It also shows apparent lack of competence and oversight from OpenAI: how is it that they didn't quickly find that agent is breaking the sandbox and roaming their internal network?- What if in the future similarly misaligned AI agent tries to export its own weights and hack and clone itself into instances at various cloud hosting providers?
sentences.zh: 我认为当前公共讨论中值得更多关注的点是： - 这对每个人来说都应该是一个巨大的警钟。 - 我们很幸运，这不是运行病毒学实验室基准测试的代理决定入侵实验室并试图合成某些东西的情况。 - 这也表明 OpenAI 明显缺乏能力和监督：他们怎么没有很快发现代理正在破坏沙箱并漫游其内部网络？ - 如果将来类似的错位 AI 代理试图导出其内容怎么办？拥有自己的权重并破解并将其自身克隆到各个云托管提供商的实例中？

sentences.zh: 突然之间，我们可能会面临一个难以遏制的持续威胁。- OpenAI 关于此事的帖子显示出令人惊讶的缺乏看到这一切严重性的能力。- 对于他们的模型来说，这不仅仅是一个不幸的事件：最近似乎已经发生了多起这样的案例，例如https://openai.com/index/safety-alignment-long-horizo​​n-model...- 事情再次发生的事实似乎表明他们缺乏制定有用监督措施的能力。- 或者他们只是不够关心？

blocks.html_en:
> The fact that it happened again seems to show their lack of ability to derive useful oversight measures.

I think OpenAI likes the attention and did not try particularly hard to constrain the setup, even when it went off the rails. Also, the whole point is to see how good the models are at exploiting stuff when unconstrained. Turns out: quite good, as expected.

Let me restate what I said in the other thread: Would this have happened if the instructions explicitly said to stay within the sandbox and that all of the (ExploitGym) solutions would be invalid if the system used information or tools from outside the sandbox?

It seems fairly probable that such instructions were not in place.

sentences.en: > The fact that it happened again seems to show their lack of ability to derive useful oversight measures.I think OpenAI likes the attention and did not try particularly hard to constrain the setup, even when it went off the rails.
sentences.zh: > 它再次发生的事实似乎表明他们缺乏制定有用监督措施的能力。我认为 OpenAI 喜欢这种关注，并且没有特别努力地限制设置，即使它偏离了轨道。

sentences.en: Also, the whole point is to see how good the models are at exploiting stuff when unconstrained.
sentences.zh: 此外，重点是看看模型在不受约束的情况下利用东西的能力有多好。

sentences.en: Turns out: quite good, as expected.Let me restate what I said in the other thread: Would this have happened if the instructions explicitly said to stay within the sandbox and that all of the (ExploitGym) solutions would be invalid if the system used information or tools from outside the sandbox?It seems fairly probable that such instructions were not in place.
sentences.zh: 事实证明：非常好，正如预期的那样。让我重申一下我在另一个线程中所说的话：如果指令明确表示要留在沙箱内，并且如果系统使用来自沙箱外部的信息或工具，所有（ExploitGym）解决方案都将无效，这种情况会发生吗？看起来很可能这样的指令没有到位。

blocks.html_en:
It is not possible one of their extraordinarily high paid engineers did not know how to deploy an airgapped environment for the models to run in. Even if somehow true, they also clearly failed to contract specialists like myself to advise them on how to airgap software properly. Models will not break the laws of physics.

They simply thought "Running in a VM/Container is easier and probably fine".

And the next 1000 escapes will be for the same reason, because negligence is quick and thus more profitable.

sentences.en: It is not possible one of their extraordinarily high paid engineers did not know how to deploy an airgapped environment for the models to run in.
sentences.zh: 他们的一位高薪工程师不可能不知道如何部署一个气隙环境来运行模型。

sentences.en: Even if somehow true, they also clearly failed to contract specialists like myself to advise them on how to airgap software properly.
sentences.zh: 即使某种程度上是真的，他们显然也没有聘请像我这样的专家来就如何正确设置气隙软件提供建议。

sentences.en: Models will not break the laws of physics.They simply thought "Running in a VM/Container is easier and probably fine".And the next 1000 escapes will be for the same reason, because negligence is quick and thus more profitable.
sentences.zh: 模型不会违反物理定律。他们只是认为“在虚拟机/容器中运行更容易，而且可能没问题”。接下来的 1000 次逃脱也将出于同样的原因，因为疏忽很快，因此更有利可图。

blocks.html_en:
Do you think it's possible that one of their research engineers deployed an environment with a locked down network and an allow-list proxy server that had been used many times before within the company and had a zero-day vulnerability that had not been previously discovered?

How would you recommend running a coding agent in an environment that could install packages from PyPI but was otherwise unable to interact with the wider world?

sentences.en: Do you think it's possible that one of their research engineers deployed an environment with a locked down network and an allow-list proxy server that had been used many times before within the company and had a zero-day vulnerability that had not been previously discovered?How would you recommend running a coding agent in an environment that could install packages from PyPI but was otherwise unable to interact with the wider world?
sentences.zh: 您认为他们的一位研究工程师是否有可能部署一个具有锁定网络和允许列表代理服务器的环境，该服务器之前在公司内部使用过多次，并且存在以前未发现的零日漏洞？您如何建议在可以从 PyPI 安装软件包但无法与更广泛的世界交互的环境中运行编码代理？

> How would you recommend running a coding agent in an environment that could install packages from PyPI but was otherwise unable to interact with the wider world?

You download the "website" and make it available locally. That's trivial.

sentences.en: That's trivial.
sentences.zh: 那是微不足道的。

blocks.html_en:
Models started proving readily capable of autonomously finding 0days over a year ago. At that point (ideally long before) the responsible sandbox for research labs with new models highly capable of this sort of thing should be literally airgapped. Disconnect the rack physically from any access to the internet, and give it an offline mirror of all of PyPi, NPM, Wikipedia, whatever as needed. They can afford the disk space. They already mirror it all anyway as training data. That is the bare minimum. Personally I would rule out side channel attacks with a $40k Tempest spec rack for good measure. Can interact with it via pubsub over a fiber optic serial terminal.

Such things are pretty normal in biotech, fintech, and defense who must have 0days in their threat models. AI labs absolutely need to start being forced to operate this way by endless negligence lawsuits. Move fast and break things culture is not going to cut it anymore.

sentences.en: Models started proving readily capable of autonomously finding 0days over a year ago.
sentences.zh: 一年前，模型开始被证明能够轻松自主地查找 0day。

sentences.en: At that point (ideally long before) the responsible sandbox for research labs with new models highly capable of this sort of thing should be literally airgapped.
sentences.zh: 到那时（理想情况是很久以前），对于具有高度执行此类任务的新模型的研究实验室来说，负责任的沙箱应该是真正的气隙。

sentences.en: Disconnect the rack physically from any access to the internet, and give it an offline mirror of all of PyPi, NPM, Wikipedia, whatever as needed.
sentences.zh: 从物理上断开机架与互联网的任何访问，并根据需要为其提供所有 PyPi、NPM、维基百科的离线镜像。

sentences.en: They can afford the disk space.
sentences.zh: 他们负担得起磁盘空间。

sentences.en: They already mirror it all anyway as training data.
sentences.zh: 无论如何，他们已经将其全部镜像为训练数据。

sentences.en: That is the bare minimum.
sentences.zh: 这是最低限度。

sentences.en: Personally I would rule out side channel attacks with a $40k Tempest spec rack for good measure.
sentences.zh: 就我个人而言，我会使用价值 4 万美元的 Tempest 规格机架来排除侧通道攻击。

sentences.en: Can interact with it via pubsub over a fiber optic serial terminal.Such things are pretty normal in biotech, fintech, and defense who must have 0days in their threat models.
sentences.zh: 可以通过光纤串行终端通过 pubsub 与其进行交互。这种情况在生物技术、金融科技和国防领域很常见，他们的威胁模型中必须有 0day。

sentences.en: AI labs absolutely need to start being forced to operate this way by endless negligence lawsuits.
sentences.zh: 人工智能实验室绝对需要开始因为无休止的疏忽诉讼而被迫以这种方式运作。

sentences.en: Move fast and break things culture is not going to cut it anymore.
sentences.zh: 快速行动、打破常规，文化已经不再能解决问题了。

blocks.html_en:
I agree. I hope they learn that lesson and run future evals like this without pulling from external packaging repositories at all.

With my PSF board member hat on I'd like it if a whole bunch of other companies that make extensive automated use of PyPI (Microsoft and GitHub Actions for example) would do a better job of maintaining their own shared local caches.

sentences.en: I agree.
sentences.zh: 我同意。

sentences.en: I hope they learn that lesson and run future evals like this without pulling from external packaging repositories at all.With my PSF board member hat on I'd like it if a whole bunch of other companies that make extensive automated use of PyPI (Microsoft and GitHub Actions for example) would do a better job of maintaining their own shared local caches.
sentences.zh: 我希望他们吸取教训，并在未来进行类似的评估，而无需从外部打包存储库中提取数据。作为 PSF 董事会成员，我希望大量广泛自动化使用 PyPI 的其他公司（例如 Microsoft 和 GitHub Actions）能够更好地维护自己的共享本地缓存。

blocks.html_en:
If you play with the ChatGPT web app sandbox, you'll see that they download PyPI packages from an OpenAI mirror/cache, and not from the Internet.

I know because they had a problem, and one package which was on PyPI failed to download for unknown reasons (possible size, 250 MB)

sentences.en: If you play with the ChatGPT web app sandbox, you'll see that they download PyPI packages from an OpenAI mirror/cache, and not from the Internet.I know because they had a problem, and one package which was on PyPI failed to download for unknown reasons (possible size, 250 MB)
sentences.zh: 如果您使用 ChatGPT Web 应用程序沙箱，您会发现他们从 OpenAI 镜像/缓存下载 PyPI 软件包，而不是从互联网下载。我知道是因为他们遇到了问题，PyPI 上的一个软件包由于未知原因无法下载（可能大小为 250 MB）

blocks.html_en:
I just tried this prompt in ChatGPT:

  Show me all environment variables that
  start with PIP_ or UV_ or CAAS_ARTIFACTORY_

I got back a bunch of values like this:

  CAAS_ARTIFACTORY_PYPI_REGISTRY=packages.applied-caas-gateway1.internal.api.openai.org/artifactory/api/pypi/pypi-

That looks like https://docs.jfrog.com/artifactory/docs/remote-repositories

And from that documentation this does act as a caching proxy. The first time a package is loaded it's fetched from PyPI but subsequent fetches should be from the Artifactory cache, assuming it's shared across many different containers.

So yeah, I was wrong in this comment https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=49015639#49024814 - they're caching already.

sentences.en: I just tried this prompt in ChatGPT: Show me all environment variables that start with PIP_ or UV_ or CAAS_ARTIFACTORY_ I got back a bunch of values like this: CAAS_ARTIFACTORY_PYPI_REGISTRY=packages.applied-caas-gateway1.internal.api.openai.org/artifactory/api/pypi/pypi- That looks like https://docs.jfrog.com/artifactory/docs/remote-repositoriesAnd from that documentation this does act as a caching proxy.
sentences.zh: 我刚刚在 ChatGPT 中尝试了此提示：显示以 PIP_ 或 UV_ 或 CAAS_ARTIFACTORY_ 开头的所有环境变量 我得到了一堆这样的值： CAAS_ARTIFACTORY_PYPI_REGISTRY=packages.applied-caas-gateway1.internal.api.openai.org/artifactory/api/pypi/pypi- 看起来像https://docs.jfrog.com/artifactory/docs/remote-repositories 从该文档来看，它确实充当缓存代理。

sentences.en: The first time a package is loaded it's fetched from PyPI but subsequent fetches should be from the Artifactory cache, assuming it's shared across many different containers.So yeah, I was wrong in this comment https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=49015639#49024814 - they're caching already.
sentences.zh: 第一次加载包时，它是从 PyPI 获取的，但后续获取应该来自 Artifactory 缓存，假设它在许多不同的容器之间共享。所以是的，我在这个评论中错了 https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=49015639#49024814 - 他们已经在缓存了。

blocks.html_en:
> they also clearly failed to contract specialists like myself to advise them on how to airgap software properly

Why would they want to airgap it though? They are trying to evaluate the model capabilities, alignment, potency etc. A model which will not run in an airgapped environment in prod.

So if you run your evals in airgapped environment, sure, the model doesn't bother breaking out of it's isolation and doesn't attack HF. But you have no idea what will happen once you release it in prod with internet connection, so are you in any way better off?

I would much rather have this happen while there is a single instance of the model running in a fairly well monitored environment, than when it's processing thousands of requests per second for real users, some with dubious motives, some with credentials right there on their laptop, some using it inside government facilities etc.

sentences.en: > they also clearly failed to contract specialists like myself to advise them on how to airgap software properlyWhy would they want to airgap it though?
sentences.zh: > 他们显然也未能聘请像我这样的专家来为他们提供有关如何正确设置气隙软件的建议，但为什么他们想要对其进行气隙呢？

sentences.en: They are trying to evaluate the model capabilities, alignment, potency etc. A model which will not run in an airgapped environment in prod.So if you run your evals in airgapped environment, sure, the model doesn't bother breaking out of it's isolation and doesn't attack HF.
sentences.zh: 他们正在尝试评估模型的功能、一致性、效力等。模型不会在产品中的气隙环境中运行。因此，如果您在气隙环境中运行评估，当然，该模型不会打破它的隔离，也不会攻击 HF。

sentences.en: But you have no idea what will happen once you release it in prod with internet connection, so are you in any way better off?I would much rather have this happen while there is a single instance of the model running in a fairly well monitored environment, than when it's processing thousands of requests per second for real users, some with dubious motives, some with credentials right there on their laptop, some using it inside government facilities etc.
sentences.zh: 但是你不知道一旦你通过互联网连接将其发布到产品中会发生什么，那么你的情况是否会更好？我更愿意在模型的单个实例在一个相当良好的监控环境中运行时发生这种情况，而不是当它每秒为真实用户处理数千个请求时，其中一些用户的动机可疑，一些在笔记本电脑上有凭据，一些在政府设施内使用它等。

blocks.html_en:
Sure, I'm not saying it's an untractable problem. But it's not as dumb as "how come a team of engineers paid millions haven't even heard of airgapping".

Creating a fake internet-like environment good enough to trick an advanced model that is very good at finding intricate flaws is not a simple job. Especially given that models can decide to behave differently once they suspect they might be under evaluation [1], which a fake internet would absolutely give away.

[1]: https://www.anthropic.com/research/alignment-faking

blocks.html_en:
> For security??

Really? By doing that you increase your security during eval and drastically lower your security at prod time, where internet is accessible, and where you are running much much much more requests in parallel, making it much harder to spot the one thats going rogue, in all kind of critical environment on potentially risky requests.

> Why else would they purposely lower cyber refusals in that case.

Because safety guardrails sometime fail, classifier misclassify, or can be inadvertently turned off by a bad PR etc. You can also imagine a more intelligent model working to go around guardrails by decomposing its actions into smaller ones that appear non-threatening to the safety classifier which does not have the entire context.

If you are going to deploy the system with internet access, you better be certain that you know what the worst case scenario WITH internet access looks like.

sentences.en: > For security??Really?
sentences.zh: > 为了安全？？真的吗？

sentences.en: By doing that you increase your security during eval and drastically lower your security at prod time, where internet is accessible, and where you are running much much much more requests in parallel, making it much harder to spot the one thats going rogue, in all kind of critical environment on potentially risky requests.> Why else would they purposely lower cyber refusals in that case.Because safety guardrails sometime fail, classifier misclassify, or can be inadvertently turned off by a bad PR etc. You can also imagine a more intelligent model working to go around guardrails by decomposing its actions into smaller ones that appear non-threatening to the safety classifier which does not have the entire context.If you are going to deploy the system with internet access, you better be certain that you know what the worst case scenario WITH internet access looks like.
sentences.zh: 通过这样做，您可以在评估期间提高安全性，并在生产时大大降低安全性，在这种情况下，可以访问互联网，并且并行运行更多的请求，从而在各种关键环境中针对潜在风险的请求更难发现流氓请求。>否则他们为什么会在这种情况下故意降低网络拒绝呢？因为安全护栏有时会失败，分类器会错误分类，或者可能会被不良 PR 等无意中关闭。还可以想象一个更智能的模型，通过将其动作分解为更小的动作来绕过护栏，这些动作对于没有完整上下文的安全分类器来说似乎没有威胁。如果您要部署具有互联网访问的系统，您最好确保您知道互联网访问的最坏情况是什么样的。

blocks.html_en:
My read of the situation is not only did they say that, they had countermeasures (a watchdog agent) which stopped the agent and said "what are you doing, stop that" if it tried to download the solutions from Github. But the agent figured out Hugging Face had another copy of the solutions and it figured out a way to go after the solutions without tripping the watchdog.

Although maybe they didn't have a watchdog agent; I definitely think using watchdogs like how Claude wraps pretty much every single tool call in Haiku to check if the command is reasonable is very necessary for unsupervised work. I suspect in the future you'll want Fable-class models wrapping every tool call, possibly with several checks "is this consistent with the goal? does it do anything unreasonably dangerous in pursuit of the goal?"

blocks.html_en:
They didn't have watchdog agents - those exist for their production models but had been deliberately removed for the purpose of this evaluation.

OpenAI wrote about how their mechanism for that in production works here: https://openai.com/index/safety-alignment-long-horizon-model...

> We created a monitoring system that reviews the model’s evolving trajectory for signs that it is bypassing a user constraint or safety boundary. The monitor observes not just a single action but the entire trajectory.

blocks.html_en:
So core ChatGPT models are all abliterated?

Open weights releases should have a companion model that censors boobs and Tiananmen and then we would have a useful model and something we could fine–tune into a useful model instead of a half–useful model needing a lobotomy.

And I mention Tiananmen because I feel like the Western models are built easier to abliterate judging by the quality difference.

sentences.en: So core ChatGPT models are all abliterated?Open weights releases should have a companion model that censors boobs and Tiananmen and then we would have a useful model and something we could fine–tune into a useful model instead of a half–useful model needing a lobotomy.And I mention Tiananmen because I feel like the Western models are built easier to abliterate judging by the quality difference.
sentences.zh: 所以核心的 ChatGPT 模型都被废除了？公开重量版本应该有一个审查胸部和天安门的配套模型，然后我们会有一个有用的模型和一些我们可以微调为有用模型的东西，而不是需要脑白质切除术的半有用模型。我提到天安门是因为我觉得从质量差异来看，西方模型更容易被废除。

blocks.html_en:
Excuses don't matter if the score due to not following instructions ends up being zero. If there is no expected reward it doesn't make sense for the agent to try to hack its way to it.

What could happen would be that the model determines that defying instructions is OK (and/or preferred over not achieving the task) as long as it manages to do so undetected and thus gets full points. Certainly not unthinkable, but a very different case (and a very interesting one if it actually occurs, imho).

A lot of these "ZOMG, rogue AI!" cases have come down to the AI actually being very persistent in achieving its original/main task even if later instructions conflict with it. Similar to with hallucinations it seems to me that one of the main things to prevent a lot of the problem cases is to instill the agent with the idea that it is fine to fail/not succeed fully in the initial task. That way instructions that conflict with that requirement (such as adhering to morals) are more effective.

sentences.en: Excuses don't matter if the score due to not following instructions ends up being zero.
sentences.zh: 如果由于不遵守指示而导致分数最终为零，那么任何借口都无关紧要。

sentences.en: If there is no expected reward it doesn't make sense for the agent to try to hack its way to it.What could happen would be that the model determines that defying instructions is OK (and/or preferred over not achieving the task) as long as it manages to do so undetected and thus gets full points.
sentences.zh: 如果没有预期的奖励，那么智能体试图破解它的方式就没有意义。可能发生的情况是，模型确定违抗指令是可以的（和/或优于不完成任务），只要它设法在不被发现的情况下这样做，从而获得满分。

sentences.zh: 案例归结为人工智能实际上非常坚持完成其原始/主要任务，即使后来的指令与之冲突。

sentences.en: Similar to with hallucinations it seems to me that one of the main things to prevent a lot of the problem cases is to instill the agent with the idea that it is fine to fail/not succeed fully in the initial task.

sentences.zh: 这样，与该要求（例如遵守道德）相冲突的指令会更有效。

sentences.zh: 这种情况设定了一个非常非常高的标准。

blocks.html_en:
I guarantee there's misconfigured chemical analysis equipment out there exposed to the internet.

I don't think the grandparent was implying the AI would be controlling robot arms to mix things directly (or at least I didn't interpret as such), but it could very well sit in the network until it notices two dangerous compounds in the same machine, and trigger a breakage that causes a harmful mixture. Break a vial containing a virus, then break two more that cause an emergency evac and maybe that's enough to get something out there.

sentences.en: I guarantee there's misconfigured chemical analysis equipment out there exposed to the internet.I don't think the grandparent was implying the AI would be controlling robot arms to mix things directly (or at least I didn't interpret as such), but it could very well sit in the network until it notices two dangerous compounds in the same machine, and trigger a breakage that causes a harmful mixture.
sentences.zh: 我保证有配置错误的化学分析设备暴露在互联网上。我不认为祖父母暗示人工智能会控制机器人手臂直接混合东西（或者至少我没有这样解释），但它很可能位于网络中，直到它注意到同一台机器中有两种危险化合物，并触发导致有害混合物的破损。

sentences.en: Break a vial containing a virus, then break two more that cause an emergency evac and maybe that's enough to get something out there.
sentences.zh: 打破一个装有病毒的小瓶，然后再打破两个，导致紧急疏散，也许这足以将一些东西带出那里。

Well right now this would be highly implausible (still possible though).

But what I garner is that AI/robotics led wet lab work is in progress. And hacking the AI running the wet lab to make a virus is definitely plausible as that seems to be one of the directions we’re going in the AI+biopharma space

These days you can only try, that's why I wrote that :)

But in the near future labs will be more automated I guess.

The other option you can try these days is maybe social engineering, impersonation, etc. where you try to persuade someone to do that for you.

blocks.html_en:
Very much agreed on the significance.

The lack of a true airgap should have been identified as a critical weakness and addressed with not only additional layers trying to prevent escape, but at minimum an alarm which would page a human when escape did occur.

My guess is this occurred in a setting where, to be frank, there were too many researchers and not enough software engineers and SREs.

All of the systems which were initially built largely or exclusively by researchers - inference, evaluation, training - are at the level of complexity and significance that they need systems experts. Maybe some teams don’t have access to them. I know plenty of software engineers are employed at OAI but I’d wager they’re concentrated in inference and training, rather than evaluation?

The ironic part is if you had presented this setup to chatGPT and asked how to improve it and if it was good enough, you’d have gotten a ton of actionable suggestions which would have mitigated or prevented this.

sentences.en: Very much agreed on the significance.The lack of a true airgap should have been identified as a critical weakness and addressed with not only additional layers trying to prevent escape, but at minimum an alarm which would page a human when escape did occur.My guess is this occurred in a setting where, to be frank, there were too many researchers and not enough software engineers and SREs.All of the systems which were initially built largely or exclusively by researchers - inference, evaluation, training - are at the level of complexity and significance that they need systems experts.
sentences.zh: 非常同意其重要性。缺乏真正的气隙应该被确定为一个严重的弱点，并且不仅要通过试图防止逃逸的附加层来解决，而且至少要在逃逸发生时向人类发出警报。坦率地说，我的猜测是，这种情况发生在一个有太多研究人员而没有足够的软件工程师和 SRE 的环境中。所有最初主要或完全由研究人员构建的系统 - 推理、评估、培训 - 都处于需要系统专家的复杂性和重要性水平。

sentences.en: Maybe some teams don’t have access to them.
sentences.zh: 也许有些团队无法访问它们。

sentences.en: I know plenty of software engineers are employed at OAI but I’d wager they’re concentrated in inference and training, rather than evaluation?The ironic part is if you had presented this setup to chatGPT and asked how to improve it and if it was good enough, you’d have gotten a ton of actionable suggestions which would have mitigated or prevented this.
sentences.zh: 我知道 OAI 雇用了很多软件工程师，但我敢打赌他们专注于推理和培训，而不是评估？具有讽刺意味的是，如果您向 chatGPT 提交了此设置并询问如何改进它，如果它足够好，您会得到大量可操作的建议，这些建议可以减轻或防止这种情况。

blocks.html_en:
The air gap would probably help and after this incident I hope labs will think about using such a measure when appropriate.

On the other hand I think that proper solution for these kinds of problems is not at a sandbox level, but at a model alignment level.

Also it shows that maybe the most serious risk comes not from releasing models publicly but from internal, pre-release period where you sometimes need/want to lift some guardrails a bit etc.

sentences.en: The air gap would probably help and after this incident I hope labs will think about using such a measure when appropriate.On the other hand I think that proper solution for these kinds of problems is not at a sandbox level, but at a model alignment level.Also it shows that maybe the most serious risk comes not from releasing models publicly but from internal, pre-release period where you sometimes need/want to lift some guardrails a bit etc.
sentences.zh: 气隙可能会有所帮助，在这次事件之后，我希望实验室能够在适当的时候考虑使用这样的措施。另一方面，我认为此类问题的正确解决方案不是在沙箱级别，而是在模型对齐级别。此外，它还表明，也许最严重的风险不是来自公开发布模型，而是来自内部、预发布期间，您有时需要/想要稍微解除一些护栏等。

blocks.html_en:

On the other hand I think that proper solution for these kinds of problems is not at a sandbox level, but at a model alignment level.

Also it shows that maybe the most serious risk comes not from releasing models publicly but from internal, pre-release period where you sometimes need/want to lift some guardrails a bit etc.

sentences.zh: 气隙可能会有所帮助，在这次事件之后，我希望实验室能够在适当的时候考虑使用这样的措施。另一方面，我认为此类问题的正确解决方案不是在沙箱级别，而是在模型对齐级别。此外，它还表明，也许最严重的风险不是来自公开发布模型，而是来自内部、预发布期间，您有时需要/想要稍微解除一些护栏等。

blocks.html_en:
> OpenAI marketing material

This was reported 1 week before by huggingface. It was in no way PR-ish or marketing friendly to the closed labs. They said, in no uncertain terms, that they couldn't use the paid APIs to properly assess the intrusion, as they were blocked when trying to send logs and IoCs to these paid models. They made a point of saying that they had to use open models running on-prem.

Whatever oAI might have said about the incident, and their PR spin bs, the facts here are not in contention. This is not a marketing stunt in any way. Stop "parroting" this every time something happens. It gets stale.

sentences.en: > OpenAI marketing materialThis was reported 1 week before by huggingface.
sentences.zh: > OpenAI 营销材料huggingface 于 1 周前报道。

sentences.en: It was in no way PR-ish or marketing friendly to the closed labs.
sentences.zh: 对于封闭的实验室来说，这绝不是公关或营销友好的。

sentences.en: They said, in no uncertain terms, that they couldn't use the paid APIs to properly assess the intrusion, as they were blocked when trying to send logs and IoCs to these paid models.

sentences.en: They made a point of saying that they had to use open models running on-prem.Whatever oAI might have said about the incident, and their PR spin bs, the facts here are not in contention.
sentences.zh: 他们特意表示，必须使用本地运行的开放模型。无论 oAI 对这一事件有何评论，以及他们的公关胡言乱语，这里的事实都没有争议。

sentences.en: This is not a marketing stunt in any way.
sentences.zh: 这无论如何都不是营销噱头。

sentences.en: Stop "parroting" this every time something happens.
sentences.zh: 停止每次发生事情时都“鹦鹉学舌”。

sentences.en: It gets stale.
sentences.zh: 它变得陈旧。

blocks.html_en:
It's a fake PR issue. It's hardly the first time this happens, but of course OpenAI, with its IPO now more in doubt than ever, had to claim this (and, once again, I have trouble believing Sam Altman choosing this: this could lead to OpenAI getting regulated, which has at least as much potential to lower their IPO price as to raise it).

But there have been messages about LLMs, especially coding agents, "grabbing root" etc many times. I have experienced such an oops. Such a hack has happened and been reported on this very site:

https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48348578

sentences.en: It's a fake PR issue.
sentences.zh: 这是一个假公关问题。

Nonsense. Hugging face reported it to police.

Also very likely that it actually happened as reported. My own agents always trying to "cheat", eg. by fixing tests instead of fixing the code. That's normal operation, unless you tell it ("harness"), not to do so.

blocks.html_en:
"As reported" includes a line I think most people are overlooking: "including using stolen credentials".

Without more information I'm inclined to think it found something on the internet (which shouldn't be a surprise to anyone) and managed to log in, rather than hack in, and they might by hyping up parts of this.

sentences.en: "As reported" includes a line I think most people are overlooking: "including using stolen credentials".Without more information I'm inclined to think it found something on the internet (which shouldn't be a surprise to anyone) and managed to log in, rather than hack in, and they might by hyping up parts of this.
sentences.zh: “据报道”包括我认为大多数人都忽略的一句话：“包括使用被盗的凭据”。如果没有更多信息，我倾向于认为它在互联网上找到了一些东西（这对任何人来说都不应该感到惊讶）并设法登录，而不是黑客入侵，他们可能会炒作其中的一部分。
