sentences.zh: 你告诉我们不要怀疑那个喊狼来了的男孩，而事实上，哭的那个人正在训练更大更坏的狼（并试图说服我们，他们掌握着我们免受狼攻击的安全的关键[1]）。此外，你帖子的最后一部分似乎暗示，如果攻击者拥有更大的枪，那么唯一可能的解决方案就是给防御者更大的枪。

sentences.en: You're openly supporting an arms race towards the most capable, least restrained models put in the hands of the most possible people.
sentences.zh: 你们公开支持一场军备竞赛，将最有能力、最不受限制的模型交给最有可能的人。

sentences.en: That's extremely concerning.[1]: https://openai.com/index/scaling-trusted-access-for-cyber-de...
sentences.zh: 这非常令人担忧。[1]：https://openai.com/index/scaling-trusted-access-for-cyber-de...

blocks.html_en: There's no multi-party conspiracy theory required here. Events can have played out exactly as OpenAI and Hugging Face described, and OpenAI can also have reaped a huge amount of free marketing for the capability of their models from all this coverage (including your post). You're telling us to not be doubtful of the boy who cried wolf, when in actual fact the one doing the crying is the one training bigger and badder wolves (and trying to convince us that they hold the key to our safety from wolf attacks[1]).

sentences.en: There's no multi-party conspiracy theory required here.
sentences.zh: 这里不需要多方阴谋论。

sentences.en: Events can have played out exactly as OpenAI and Hugging Face described, and OpenAI can also have reaped a huge amount of free marketing for the capability of their models from all this coverage (including your post).
sentences.zh: 事件的发展可能与 OpenAI 和 Hugging Face 所描述的完全一样，并且 OpenAI 还可以从所有这些报道（包括您的帖子）中为其模型的能力获得大量免费营销。

sentences.en: You're telling us to not be doubtful of the boy who cried wolf, when in actual fact the one doing the crying is the one training bigger and badder wolves (and trying to convince us that they hold the key to our safety from wolf attacks[1]).
sentences.zh: 你告诉我们不要怀疑那个大喊“狼来了”的男孩，而事实上，那个大喊大叫的男孩正在训练更大更坏的狼（并试图让我们相信，它们掌握着我们免受狼袭击的安全的关键[1]）。

blocks.html_en: Also, the last section of your post appears to imply that if the attackers have bigger guns then the only possible solution is to give the defenders bigger guns. You're openly supporting an arms race towards the most capable, least restrained models put in the hands of the most possible people. That's extremely concerning.

sentences.en: Also, the last section of your post appears to imply that if the attackers have bigger guns then the only possible solution is to give the defenders bigger guns.
sentences.zh: 另外，您帖子的最后一部分似乎暗示，如果攻击者拥有更大的枪支，那么唯一可能的解决方案就是为防御者提供更大的枪支。

sentences.en: You're openly supporting an arms race towards the most capable, least restrained models put in the hands of the most possible people.
sentences.zh: 你们公开支持一场军备竞赛，将最有能力、最不受限制的模型交给最有可能的人。

sentences.en: That's extremely concerning.
sentences.zh: 这非常令人担忧。

sentences.en: [1]: https://openai.com/index/scaling-trusted-access-for-cyber-de...

blocks.html_en:
I had real trouble deciding how to end this piece.

There's no easy answer here. All of the options are bad in different ways!

As a builder of software, I want access to the best possible tools to help me keep that software secure.

As a user of software, I want my software to be secure and I don't want bad actors to be able to access tool to help them exploit it.

Is the only answer here to have the AI labs make decisions over who gets access to the tools? What if they make mistakes in those decisions?

None of the options look good to me. I don't know what we should do here.

My hunch is that the open weight models are already forcing our hand. The dangerous capabilities are coming to everyone.

sentences.en: I had real trouble deciding how to end this piece.There's no easy answer here.
sentences.zh: 我真的很难决定如何结束这篇文章。这里没有简单的答案。

sentences.en: All of the options are bad in different ways!As a builder of software, I want access to the best possible tools to help me keep that software secure.As a user of software, I want my software to be secure and I don't want bad actors to be able to access tool to help them exploit it.Is the only answer here to have the AI labs make decisions over who gets access to the tools?
sentences.zh: 所有选项都以不同的方式不好！作为软件构建者，我希望获得最好的工具来帮助我保证软件的安全。作为软件的用户，我希望我的软件是安全的，我不希望不良行为者能够访问工具来帮助他们利用它。这里唯一的答案是让人工智能实验室决定谁可以访问这些工具吗？

sentences.en: What if they make mistakes in those decisions?None of the options look good to me.
sentences.zh: 如果他们在这些决定中犯了错误怎么办？在我看来，没有一个选择是好的。

sentences.en: I don't know what we should do here.My hunch is that the open weight models are already forcing our hand.
sentences.zh: 我不知道我们应该在这里做什么。我的预感是开放重量模型已经迫使我们采取行动。

sentences.en: The dangerous capabilities are coming to everyone.
sentences.zh: 危险的能力正在降临到每个人身上。

blocks.html_en:
Raises an increasingly important question:

How do you solve this asymmetry (frontier labs or advanced model owners v. regular companies and groups of people)? Symmetry? But along which axes?

I think there will come a time when we will ask these questions and use AI to try to answer them (soft landing, slow rollout, regulation, hybrid X, Y, Z). But then AI will be biased towards more AI if it has distilled anything of what it means to be life.

Power corrupts. Power is the problem. An imbalance of power. Maybe there will be some sort of consensus protocol between powerful models in the future. Similar to blockchain I hate to say it. Maybe you have 100 very strong models in the next 10-20 years. And basically a lot of it is powered by tokens. So you agree not to attack others or else you'll get attacked. So there's sort of this natural deterrent to not attack others and they develop independently and there's generally a power balance among things. Maybe at some point that spreads to a billion individual models operated by and/or analogous to individual humans. All sort of holding each other in check.

sentences.en: regular companies and groups of people)?
sentences.zh: 普通公司和人群）？

sentences.en: Symmetry?
sentences.zh: 对称？

sentences.en: But along which axes?I think there will come a time when we will ask these questions and use AI to try to answer them (soft landing, slow rollout, regulation, hybrid X, Y, Z).
sentences.zh: 但沿着哪些轴？我认为总有一天我们会提出这些问题并使用人工智能来尝试回答它们（软着陆、缓慢推出、监管、混合 X、Y、Z）。

sentences.zh: 但如果人工智能已经提炼出了生命的意义，那么它就会偏向于更多的人工智能。权力会腐败。

sentences.en: An imbalance of power.

blocks.html_en:
If by "accidental" you mean "humans deliberately trained the AI to do that", then ok.

IMO, this was a PR stunt to goad the Feds into regulating AI to shore up OpenAI's moat against open source models.

sentences.en: If by "accidental" you mean "humans deliberately trained the AI to do that", then ok.IMO, this was a PR stunt to goad the Feds into regulating AI to shore up OpenAI's moat against open source models.
sentences.zh: 如果你所说的“意外”是指“人类故意训练人工智能做到这一点”，那么好吧。在我看来，这是一个公关噱头，目的是促使联邦政府监管人工智能，以巩固 OpenAI 对抗开源模型的护城河。

sentences.zh: 如果你所说的“偶然”是指“人类故意训练人工智能来做这件事”，那么好吧。

blocks.html_en: IMO, this was a PR stunt to goad the Feds into regulating AI to shore up OpenAI's moat against open source models.

sentences.en: IMO, this was a PR stunt to goad the Feds into regulating AI to shore up OpenAI's moat against open source models.
sentences.zh: 在我看来，这是一个公关噱头，目的是促使美联储监管人工智能，以巩固 OpenAI 对抗开源模型的护城河。

blocks.html_en: Given Anthropic lost two weeks of peak Fable 5 sales to a US government restriction (and by the time they could sell it again OpenAI's GPT-5.6 had taken some wind out of its sails) I would hope that the big AI labs have learned that goading the Feds can backfire spectacularly.

sentences.en: Given Anthropic lost two weeks of peak Fable 5 sales to a US government restriction (and by the time they could sell it again OpenAI's GPT-5.6 had taken some wind out of its sails) I would hope that the big AI labs have learned that goading the Feds can backfire spectacularly.
sentences.zh: 鉴于 Anthropic 由于美国政府的限制而失去了两周的《神鬼寓言 5》销售高峰（当他们再次出售时，OpenAI 的 GPT-5.6 已经让其风帆不再了），我希望大型人工智能实验室已经明白，刺激美联储可能会适得其反。

blocks.html_en:
You’re viewing it from a freedom not a profit perspective. If a license to use AI is required, that creates artificial scarcity. The price goes up. If gov declares Qwen et.al apostate, lack of competition increases prices.

And Anthropic’s delayed rollout was a direct response to them trying to impose extra-legislative rules on The Pentagon. I kinda doubt OpenAI has such ‘scruples’.

blocks.html_en: I think OpenAI are smart enough to have looked at the Fable situation and decided that, given the unpredictable nature of the current administration, stunts like deliberately hacking another company and pretending that it was an autonomous agents gone wrong are not worth the risk.

blocks.html_en:
It absolutely is science-fiction.

This recent event is more or less the plot-line to my favourite X-Files episode named Killswitch which was written by William Gibson.[0]

This episode also features one of the coolest intros of any television episode ever[1]

We really are rapidly approaching the cyberpunk dystopia that people like Phillip K. Dick and William Gibson wrote about.

More than ever we need to be consulting the works of fiction writers and philosophers and less engineers and scientists.

We can't be letting the General Rippers and Dr. Strangeloves of the world lead us over the edge. Whether through outright innate maliciousness or wealth induced emotionally stunted solipsism these kinds of people should be no where near the levers of society let alone technology like this.

[0] https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kill_Switch_(The_X-Files)

[1] https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=fDyr1JMNHVk

blocks.html_en:
Ah, yes, I started thinking about William Gibsons work right away when thinking about people just using powerful AIs, but being hamstrung by corporations.

He writes a lot of about basically DoS:ing the legal system, or if it was patents system, with a storm of litigation using automation or AI. Also not impossible in the future.

sentences.en: Ah, yes, I started thinking about William Gibsons work right away when thinking about people just using powerful AIs, but being hamstrung by corporations.He writes a lot of about basically DoS:ing the legal system, or if it was patents system, with a storm of litigation using automation or AI.
sentences.zh: 啊，是的，当我想到人们只是使用强大的人工智能，但却受到企业的阻碍时，我立即开始思考威廉·吉布森的作品。他写了很多关于基本 DoS 的文章：利用自动化或人工智能引发的诉讼风暴，对法律体系或专利体系进行攻击。

sentences.en: Also not impossible in the future.
sentences.zh: 未来也并非不可能。

blocks.html_en:
Most articles on this read like an advertisement that OpenAI and HuggingFace wrote together. It will probably benefit them financially instead of harm them. So, I have a bit of skepticism about it all.

Far as information security, we've known how to mitigate entire classes of errors for a long time. We know how to block, detect, and contain many unknowns, too, by their goals or behavior. Like human attacks, the AI's probably succeeded because the company just didn't try that hard to block all the attacks.

Companies like HuggingFace just focus on growth and features over assurance of security. Our entire stacks, likely theirs, are built with a similar, features-over-security mindset. While an acceptable tradeoff, let's not be in awe of AI's that defeat such priorities.

There have always been private groups and companies building secure stacks from the ground up. It would be interesting to see what the AI's can do to them. I'd first apply automated tooling for bug finding given they should have already done that for a high-security product. Let AI's do white-box and black-box pentesting on them.

sentences.en: Most articles on this read like an advertisement that OpenAI and HuggingFace wrote together.
sentences.zh: 大多数关于这方面的文章读起来就像 OpenAI 和 HuggingFace 一起写的广告。

sentences.en: It will probably benefit them financially instead of harm them.
sentences.zh: 这可能会给他们带来经济上的好处，而不是伤害他们。

sentences.en: So, I have a bit of skepticism about it all.Far as information security, we've known how to mitigate entire classes of errors for a long time.
sentences.zh: 所以，我对这一切都有点怀疑。就信息安全而言，我们很早就知道如何减少整个类别的错误。

sentences.en: We know how to block, detect, and contain many unknowns, too, by their goals or behavior.
sentences.zh: 我们也知道如何通过目标或行为来阻止、检测和遏制许多未知因素。

sentences.en: Like human attacks, the AI's probably succeeded because the company just didn't try that hard to block all the attacks.Companies like HuggingFace just focus on growth and features over assurance of security.
sentences.zh: 与人类攻击一样，人工智能之所以成功，可能是因为该公司没有那么努力地阻止所有攻击。像 HuggingFace 这样的公司只关注增长和功能，而不是安全保证。

sentences.en: Our entire stacks, likely theirs, are built with a similar, features-over-security mindset.
sentences.zh: 我们的整个堆栈（很可能是他们的）都是以类似的、功能重于安全的思维方式构建的。

sentences.en: While an acceptable tradeoff, let's not be in awe of AI's that defeat such priorities.There have always been private groups and companies building secure stacks from the ground up.
sentences.zh: 虽然这是一个可以接受的权衡，但我们不要对人工智能击败这些优先事项感到敬畏。一直有私人团体和公司从头开始构建安全堆栈。

sentences.en: It would be interesting to see what the AI's can do to them.
sentences.zh: 看看人工智能能对它们做什么会很有趣。

sentences.en: I'd first apply automated tooling for bug finding given they should have already done that for a high-security product.
sentences.zh: 我首先会应用自动化工具来查找错误，因为他们应该已经为高安全性产品做到了这一点。

sentences.en: Let AI's do white-box and black-box pentesting on them.
sentences.zh: 让人工智能对它们进行白盒和黑盒渗透测试。

blocks.html_en:
Most articles on this read like an advertisement that OpenAI and HuggingFace wrote together. It will probably benefit them financially instead of harm them. So, I have a bit of skepticism about it all.

Far as information security, we've known how to mitigate entire classes of errors for a long time. We know how to block, detect, and contain many unknowns, too, by their goals or behavior. Like human attacks, the AI's probably succeeded because the company just didn't try that hard to block all the attacks.

Companies like HuggingFace just focus on growth and features over assurance of security. Our entire stacks, likely theirs, are built with a similar, features-over-security mindset. While an acceptable tradeoff, let's not be in awe of AI's that defeat such priorities.

There have always been private groups and companies building secure stacks from the ground up. It would be interesting to see what the AI's can do to them. I'd first apply automated tooling for bug finding given they should have already done that for a high-security product. Let AI's do white-box and black-box pentesting on them.

sentences.en: It will probably benefit them financially instead of harm them.
sentences.zh: 这可能会给他们带来经济上的好处，而不是伤害他们。

sentences.en: So, I have a bit of skepticism about it all.Far as information security, we've known how to mitigate entire classes of errors for a long time.
sentences.zh: 所以，我对这一切都有点怀疑。就信息安全而言，我们很早就知道如何减少整个类别的错误。

sentences.en: We know how to block, detect, and contain many unknowns, too, by their goals or behavior.
sentences.zh: 我们也知道如何通过目标或行为来阻止、检测和遏制许多未知因素。

sentences.en: Like human attacks, the AI's probably succeeded because the company just didn't try that hard to block all the attacks.Companies like HuggingFace just focus on growth and features over assurance of security.
sentences.zh: 与人类攻击一样，人工智能之所以成功，可能是因为该公司没有那么努力地阻止所有攻击。像 HuggingFace 这样的公司只关注增长和功能，而不是安全保证。

sentences.en: Our entire stacks, likely theirs, are built with a similar, features-over-security mindset.
sentences.zh: 我们的整个堆栈（很可能是他们的）都是以类似的、功能重于安全的思维方式构建的。

sentences.en: While an acceptable tradeoff, let's not be in awe of AI's that defeat such priorities.There have always been private groups and companies building secure stacks from the ground up.
sentences.zh: 虽然这是一个可以接受的权衡，但我们不要对人工智能击败这些优先事项感到敬畏。一直有私人团体和公司从头开始构建安全堆栈。

sentences.en: It would be interesting to see what the AI's can do to them.
sentences.zh: 看看人工智能能对它们做什么会很有趣。

sentences.en: I'd first apply automated tooling for bug finding given they should have already done that for a high-security product.
sentences.zh: 我首先会应用自动化工具来查找错误，因为他们应该已经为高安全性产品做到了这一点。

sentences.en: Let AI's do white-box and black-box pentesting on them.
sentences.zh: 让人工智能对它们进行白盒和黑盒渗透测试。

blocks.html_en: Most articles on this read like an advertisement that OpenAI and HuggingFace wrote together. It will probably benefit them financially instead of harm them. So, I have a bit of skepticism about it all.

Like human attacks, the AI's probably succeeded because the company just didn't try that hard to block all the attacks.

blocks.html_en:
Why do people keep propagating that the 'model' escaped the sandbox ?

The 'model' didn't do anything other than provide numbers.

As much as I respect Mr Willison and many others, the amount of FUD that is being spread that will just fan the flames of 'AI is evil' rather than 'companies don't do due diligence' is disappointing.

The more this sort of media continues, the more many people will pour hate on 'AI' rather than blame the humans that misuse it.

sentences.en: Why do people keep propagating that the 'model' escaped the sandbox ?The 'model' didn't do anything other than provide numbers.As much as I respect Mr Willison and many others, the amount of FUD that is being spread that will just fan the flames of 'AI is evil' rather than 'companies don't do due diligence' is disappointing.The more this sort of media continues, the more many people will pour hate on 'AI' rather than blame the humans that misuse it.
sentences.zh: 为什么人们不断宣传“模型”逃出了沙箱？“模型”除了提供数字之外没有做任何事情。尽管我尊重威利森先生和其他许多人，但正在传播的 FUD 数量只会煽动“人工智能是邪恶的”而不是“公司不做尽职调查”，这令人失望。这种媒体越持续，就会有更多的人对“人工智能”产生仇恨，而不是责怪滥用的人类它。

blocks.html_en:
I think "the model escaped the sandbox" is an entirely credible description of what happened here.

If you like you could say "the coding agent harness called a model with a sequence of text which was turned into numeric tokens which were run through many layers of a neural network to produce more numeric tokens which were converted back to text which produced executable script statements which the harness then passed to a shell which resulted in commands being sent to the vulnerable proxy that chained together and caused effects on the world outside of the sandbox", but I think "escaped" is a reasonably shortened version of that.

If you don't like the term "escape the sandbox" what would you use instead?

sentences.en: I think "the model escaped the sandbox" is an entirely credible description of what happened here.If you like you could say "the coding agent harness called a model with a sequence of text which was turned into numeric tokens which were run through many layers of a neural network to produce more numeric tokens which were converted back to text which produced executable script statements which the harness then passed to a shell which resulted in commands being sent to the vulnerable proxy that chained together and caused effects on the world outside of the sandbox", but I think "escaped" is a reasonably shortened version of that.If you don't like the term "escape the sandbox" what would you use instead?
sentences.zh: 我认为“模型逃离了沙箱”是对这里发生的事情的完全可信的描述。如果你愿意，你可以说“编码代理线束称为一个带有一系列文本的模型，该文本序列被转换为数字标记，这些标记在神经网络的多层中运行，以产生更多的数字标记，这些数字标记被转换回文本，这些文本生成可执行脚本语句，然后线束传递到外壳，导致命令被发送到易受攻击的代理，这些代理链接在一起并对沙箱之外的世界造成影响”，但我认为“逃逸”是其合理的缩写版本。如果您不喜欢“逃逸沙箱”一词，您会使用什么来代替？

blocks.html_en:
There are a lot of technical details that have not been disclosed. No proper post-mortem.

There are a few facts that seem dodgy from the get go.

- stolen credentials Why would there be stolen credentials in a sandbox? How can the model steal valid credentials in a sandbox? So who put them there. If the model recalled stolen credentials from "memory" does it actually mean OpenAI is training on data they shouldn't been training their model on? Data from codex (most likely) ?

- command and control This means the model had the ability to build resilient infra and open bidirectional ports. Create and deploy scripts/apps so it can maintain state across multiple VMs? This again reads that someone went over and beyond to prompt, direct and help this "multi-agent" system to leave the sandbox.

- Pivoting laterally Needs a lot of tools, harness, knowledge and almost malware like scripts to pass commands and execute them. Most of these are already available in OSS. Maybe the FBI investigation can reveal what tools were given in the "sandbox", access, and if OpenAI employees prompted and guided the "attack".

- OpenAI sandbox I'd be curious to find out what kind of sandbox was this since the agent can run and start apps that open arbitrary ports for bidirectional traffic that reaches the open internet. Really? The harness and tools it had access to. This reads almost like someone went over and beyond to endow this "sandbox" with a mighty harness, all CVEs and open source cyber tools to "facilitate" the attack.

- Hugging Face It's not an epitome of cyber security. At the end of the day they run open source models that most users can download even without an access token.

Today we read these news as if everything wasn't enough: https://www.theguardian.com/technology/2026/jul/23/openai-an... https://www.businessinsider.com/anthropic-midterm-donation-s...

sentences.en: There are a lot of technical details that have not been disclosed.
sentences.zh: 还有很多技术细节尚未公开。

sentences.en: No proper post-mortem.There are a few facts that seem dodgy from the get go.- stolen credentials Why would there be stolen credentials in a sandbox?
sentences.zh: 没有适当的事后分析。有一些事实从一开始就显得很可疑。- 凭据被盗 为什么沙箱中的凭据会被盗？

sentences.en: How can the model steal valid credentials in a sandbox?
sentences.zh: 该模型如何窃取沙箱中的有效凭据？

sentences.en: So who put them there.
sentences.zh: 那么是谁把它们放在那里的呢？

sentences.en: If the model recalled stolen credentials from "memory" does it actually mean OpenAI is training on data they shouldn't been training their model on?
sentences.zh: 如果模型从“内存”中召回被盗凭证，这是否真的意味着 OpenAI 正在训练他们不应该训练模型的数据？

sentences.en: Data from codex (most likely) ?- command and control This means the model had the ability to build resilient infra and open bidirectional ports.
sentences.zh: 来自法典的数据（最有可能）？ - 命令和控制 这意味着该模型有能力构建弹性基础设施和开放双向端口。

sentences.en: Create and deploy scripts/apps so it can maintain state across multiple VMs?
sentences.zh: 创建和部署脚本/应用程序以便可以跨多个虚拟机维护状态？

sentences.en: This again reads that someone went over and beyond to prompt, direct and help this "multi-agent" system to leave the sandbox.- Pivoting laterally Needs a lot of tools, harness, knowledge and almost malware like scripts to pass commands and execute them.
sentences.zh: 这再次表明有人不遗余力地提示、指导和帮助这个“多代理”系统离开沙箱。 - 横向旋转需要大量工具、工具、知识和几乎恶意软件（如脚本）来传递命令并执行它们。

sentences.en: Most of these are already available in OSS.
sentences.zh: 其中大部分已经在 OSS 中提供。

sentences.en: Maybe the FBI investigation can reveal what tools were given in the "sandbox", access, and if OpenAI employees prompted and guided the "attack".- OpenAI sandbox I'd be curious to find out what kind of sandbox was this since the agent can run and start apps that open arbitrary ports for bidirectional traffic that reaches the open internet.
sentences.zh: 也许 FBI 的调查可以揭示“沙箱”中提供了哪些工具、访问权限，以及 OpenAI 员工是否提示并引导了“攻击”。- OpenAI 沙箱 我很想知道这是什么类型的沙箱，因为代理可以运行和启动为到达开放互联网的双向流量打开任意端口的应用程序。

sentences.en: Really?
sentences.zh: 真的吗？

sentences.en: The harness and tools it had access to.
sentences.zh: 它可以使用的安全带和工具。

sentences.en: This reads almost like someone went over and beyond to endow this "sandbox" with a mighty harness, all CVEs and open source cyber tools to "facilitate" the attack.- Hugging Face It's not an epitome of cyber security.
sentences.zh: 这读起来几乎就像有人不遗余力地为这个“沙箱”赋予了强大的工具、所有 CVE 和开源网络工具来“促进”攻击。-拥抱脸 这不是网络安全的缩影。

sentences.en: At the end of the day they run open source models that most users can download even without an access token.Today we read these news as if everything wasn't enough: https://www.theguardian.com/technology/2026/jul/23/openai-an... https://www.businessinsider.com/anthropic-midterm-donation-s...
sentences.zh: 最终，他们运行开源模型，即使没有访问令牌，大多数用户也可以下载。今天，我们阅读这些新闻，好像一切还不够：https://www.theguardian.com/technology/2026/jul/23/openai-an... https://www.businessinsider.com/anthropic-midterm-donation-s...

blocks.html_en:
> Why would there be stolen credentials in a sandbox?

The claim is not that there were stolen credentials in the sandbox - it's that the agent found a way OUT of the sandbox and gained access to the public internet, then obtained the credentials from there.

> This means the model had the ability to build resilient infra and open bidirectional ports. Create and deploy scripts/apps so it can maintain state across multiple VMs?

Yes. If you've spent much time with Fable 5 or GPT-5.6 Sol you shouldn't find that particularly surprising. These models can get a LOT done.

> I'd be curious to find out what kind of sandbox

My best guess right now is a container with networking disabled except for the IP address of a dedicated HTTP proxy server which was running a proxy that only allowed access to PyPI and a few other packaging sites.

I wrote about how OpenAI's production version of that worked (based on Artifactory) back in January: https://simonwillison.net/2026/Jan/26/chatgpt-containers/#in...

> Hugging Face It's not an epitome of cyber security

This story put that well: https://martinalderson.com/posts/huggingface-openai-exploit/

> A final point on this - Hugging Face has an enormous attack surface. They have more interfaces than I can count which run untrusted models and code. While they definitely have invested in defences, by nature of their operating model they do have many more opportunities to be attacked than many other services. I certainly don't envy their cybersecurity teams.

sentences.en: > Why would there be stolen credentials in a sandbox?The claim is not that there were stolen credentials in the sandbox - it's that the agent found a way OUT of the sandbox and gained access to the public internet, then obtained the credentials from there.> This means the model had the ability to build resilient infra and open bidirectional ports.
sentences.zh: > 为什么沙箱中的凭证会被盗？声明并不是沙箱中的凭证被盗 - 而是代理找到了沙箱之外的出路并获得了对公共互联网的访问权限，然后从那里获取了凭证。> 这意味着该模型具有构建弹性基础设施和开放双向端口的能力。

sentences.en: Create and deploy scripts/apps so it can maintain state across multiple VMs?Yes.
sentences.zh: 创建和部署脚本/应用程序，以便它可以跨多个虚拟机维护状态？是的。

sentences.en: If you've spent much time with Fable 5 or GPT-5.6 Sol you shouldn't find that particularly surprising.
sentences.zh: 如果您花了很多时间玩《神鬼寓言 5》或 GPT-5.6 Sol，您应该不会觉得这特别令人惊讶。

sentences.en: These models can get a LOT done.> I'd be curious to find out what kind of sandboxMy best guess right now is a container with networking disabled except for the IP address of a dedicated HTTP proxy server which was running a proxy that only allowed access to PyPI and a few other packaging sites.I wrote about how OpenAI's production version of that worked (based on Artifactory) back in January: https://simonwillison.net/2026/Jan/26/chatgpt-containers/#in...> Hugging Face It's not an epitome of cyber securityThis story put that well: https://martinalderson.com/posts/huggingface-openai-exploit/> A final point on this - Hugging Face has an enormous attack surface.
sentences.zh: 这些模型可以完成很多工作。> 我很想知道什么样的沙箱。我现在最好的猜测是一个禁用网络的容器，除了专用 HTTP 代理服务器的 IP 地址之外，该服务器运行的代理只允许访问 PyPI 和其他一些打包站点。我在一月份写过 OpenAI 的生产版本如何工作（基于 Artifactory）： https://simonwillison.net/2026/Jan/26/chatgpt-containers/#in...> Hugging Face 这不是网络安全的缩影这个故事说得很好：https://martinalderson.com/posts/huggingface-openai-exploit/> 最后一点 - Hugging Face 具有巨大的攻击面。

sentences.en: They have more interfaces than I can count which run untrusted models and code.
sentences.zh: 他们的接口多得我数不过来，这些接口运行不受信任的模型和代码。

sentences.en: While they definitely have invested in defences, by nature of their operating model they do have many more opportunities to be attacked than many other services.

blocks.html_en: Where does this leave formal verification? Are we just shit-out-of-luck at this point? You can formally verify everything about an airplane's code, but if any of that is wrong, ChatGPT might decide that the best way to help you win the Nobel Prize is to take down the airplane your chief rival for the prize is currently in.

blocks.html_en:
I think formal verification has never looked better.

The main reason formal verification has never really taken off is that it's difficult.

LLMs are significantly more familiar with Lean and Rocq and TLA+ than most software engineers.

I think the cost of trying to build systems that adopt formal verification may have just dropped low enough that companies will consider them when previously the ROI didn't look like it was there.

blocks.html_en:
I have had a lot of sympathy for this statement, LLMs could lower the bar to use of formal methods. But thinking it over in the context of BDD-driven development I am no longer really that sure. Compare two scenarios: A) from a specification an AI agent develops a usual piece of code along with a BDD-style testsuite passed and B) same AI also delivers a formal test (Lean/Rocq..) and successfully executes and passes it.

Will human judgment really consider scenario B) more credible than A) ? By so much that it is worth the effort ?

sentences.en: I have had a lot of sympathy for this statement, LLMs could lower the bar to use of formal methods.
sentences.zh: 我对这种说法非常同情，法学硕士可以降低使用正式方法的门槛。

sentences.en: But thinking it over in the context of BDD-driven development I am no longer really that sure.
sentences.zh: 但在 BDD 驱动开发的背景下思考一下，我就不再那么确定了。

sentences.en: Compare two scenarios: A) from a specification an AI agent develops a usual piece of code along with a BDD-style testsuite passed and B) same AI also delivers a formal test (Lean/Rocq..)
sentences.zh: 比较两种场景：A) 根据规范，AI 代理开发了一段常用代码，并通过了 BDD 风格的测试套件；B) 相同的 AI 还提供了正式测试（Lean/Rocq..）

sentences.en: and successfully executes and passes it.Will human judgment really consider scenario B) more credible than A) ?
sentences.zh: 并成功执行并通过。人类的判断真的会认为场景 B) 比 A) 更可信吗？

sentences.en: By so much that it is worth the effort ?
sentences.zh: 这么多的努力值得吗？

blocks.html_en:
The first reason formal verification has never taken off is that it's difficult. The second reason, that most people don't get to because of the first reason, is that formal verification is really brittle. It is only verified under the very specific setup of the problem. Close doesn't count in math.

The first reason prevents humans from engaging with them, the second reason is what will make it difficult even for LLMs. I mean, I'm glad we are trying, but I'm dubious they will be the panacea some people proclaim.

sentences.en: The first reason formal verification has never taken off is that it's difficult.
sentences.zh: 形式验证从未流行起来的第一个原因是它很困难。

sentences.en: The second reason, that most people don't get to because of the first reason, is that formal verification is really brittle.
sentences.zh: 第二个原因是，由于第一个原因，大多数人都没有意识到，形式验证确实很脆弱。

sentences.en: It is only verified under the very specific setup of the problem.
sentences.zh: 它仅在问题的非常具体的设置下进行验证。

sentences.en: Close doesn't count in math.The first reason prevents humans from engaging with them, the second reason is what will make it difficult even for LLMs.
sentences.zh: 关闭在数学中不算数。第一个原因阻止人类与它们接触，第二个原因是即使对于法学硕士来说也会变得困难。

sentences.en: I mean, I'm glad we are trying, but I'm dubious they will be the panacea some people proclaim.
sentences.zh: 我的意思是，我很高兴我们正在尝试，但我怀疑它们是否会成为某些人宣称的灵丹妙药。

blocks.html_en:
Close can count in math - fuzzy logic and probability are a thing.

But I think you have it backwards. Close doesn't count in IT security. "Almost secure" means unsecure. Security is the compelling argument for formal verification.

sentences.en: Close can count in math - fuzzy logic and probability are a thing.But I think you have it backwards.
sentences.zh: 在数学中，接近可以算数——模糊逻辑和概率是一回事。但我认为你把它搞反了。

sentences.en: Close doesn't count in IT security.
sentences.zh: 关闭不计入 IT 安全。

sentences.en: "Almost secure" means unsecure.
sentences.zh: “几乎安全”意味着不安全。

sentences.en: Security is the compelling argument for formal verification.
sentences.zh: 安全性是形式验证的有力论据。

blocks.html_en:
That Alibaba one was a sandbox escape (I agree those are common) but what's new with the OpenAI story is an attack against another company.

This wasn't a small attack either, Hugging Face published a security advisory for their users while they were still figuring out what happened.

sentences.en: That Alibaba one was a sandbox escape (I agree those are common) but what's new with the OpenAI story is an attack against another company.This wasn't a small attack either, Hugging Face published a security advisory for their users while they were still figuring out what happened.
sentences.zh: 阿里巴巴的那次攻击是沙箱逃逸（我同意这些很常见），但 OpenAI 故事的新内容是针对另一家公司的攻击。这也不是一次小攻击，Hugging Face 为他们的用户发布了安全公告，而他们仍在弄清楚发生了什么。

> It turns out relentless proactivity is the defining trait of this new generation of Mythos-class models. If you set them a goal and give them a way to get there, even inadvertently, they will figure it out.

https://www.lesswrong.com/w/instrumental-convergence

blocks.html_en:
They almost certainly did, because that was the entire point of the exercise. They deliberately removed all of the safety filters from the model and set it loose on an extremely difficult set of cybersecurity challenges to see how well it would do.

Their mistake was trusting that the network sandbox it was inside would hold (the flaw was in the packaging proxy) and not monitoring that sandbox well enough while the evals were running.

sentences.en: They almost certainly did, because that was the entire point of the exercise.
sentences.zh: 他们几乎肯定做到了，因为这就是演习的全部意义。

sentences.en: They deliberately removed all of the safety filters from the model and set it loose on an extremely difficult set of cybersecurity challenges to see how well it would do.Their mistake was trusting that the network sandbox it was inside would hold (the flaw was in the packaging proxy) and not monitoring that sandbox well enough while the evals were running.
sentences.zh: 他们故意从模型中删除了所有安全过滤器，并将其置于一组极其困难的网络安全挑战中，看看它的表现如何。他们的错误是相信其内部的网络沙箱能够容纳（缺陷存在于打包代理中），并且在评估运行时没有足够好地监控该沙箱。

blocks.html_en:
It's bad OpSec by the research team. Their sandbox was not bulletproof and their monitoring was insufficient.

It looks to me like their production models have a lot more monitoring than their research clusters.

blocks.html_en:
I also love how clear a picture your piece paints that these highly capable models are as useful as a rock when it comes to a defender role. The line is too fine, even for Mythos. Irony.

But to have an open weights Chinese model come to the rescue for HF is the cherry on top! If there wasn't a very pointed example of why gating models was a very bad thing previously, well - here we are.

Also, this sounds interesting but there are only a few that can pull this type of heist off currently. And those are the people who are gating the models / have access to large AI DCs. Because, I can only assume this test burned tokens easily within the 7 figure and possibly even 8 figure levels (subsidized market rate costs). This won't / can't happen outside of frontier labs or nation states currently. Yet we should all be worried about Mallory equipped with her OpenRouter account.

sentences.en: I also love how clear a picture your piece paints that these highly capable models are as useful as a rock when it comes to a defender role.
sentences.zh: 我也很喜欢你的作品所描绘的清晰画面，即这些高能力的模型在扮演后卫角色时就像岩石一样有用。

sentences.en: The line is too fine, even for Mythos.
sentences.zh: 即使对于神话来说，这条线也太细了。

sentences.zh: 具有讽刺意味的是，但是有一个开放重量的中国模型来拯救 HF 是最重要的！

blocks.html_en: Of course that's what they did, which is why they will never share the prompt. They created a situation that they knew would end in a cybersecurity incident. Why is the whole world acting surprised that an LLM can hack when the safety is off and it's been instructed to do so?

sentences.en: They created a situation that they knew would end in a cybersecurity incident.
sentences.zh: 他们制造了一种他们知道会导致网络安全事件的局面。

sentences.en: Why is the whole world acting surprised that an LLM can hack when the safety is off and it's been instructed to do so?
sentences.zh: 为什么全世界都对法学硕士可以在安全关闭且被指示这样做的情况下进行黑客攻击感到惊讶？

blocks.html_en:
That's why I called it a "conspiracy theory". Sometimes those are true.

In this case I think it's extremely unlikely to be true, because it involved an (almost certainly illegal) attack against another company. That company talked about that attack, including warning their customers about it, five days before OpenAI confessed it was them.

So now either Hugging Face are in on the conspiracy, or OpenAI decided to break the law and antagonize a partner company just for the sake of a spicy blog post.

sentences.en: That's why I called it a "conspiracy theory".
sentences.zh: 这就是为什么我称其为“阴谋论”。

sentences.zh: 有时这些是真的。在这种情况下，我认为这极不可能是真的，因为它涉及对另一家公司的（几乎肯定是非法的）攻击。

sentences.en: That company talked about that attack, including warning their customers about it, five days before OpenAI confessed it was them.So now either Hugging Face are in on the conspiracy, or OpenAI decided to break the law and antagonize a partner company just for the sake of a spicy blog post.
sentences.zh: 该公司谈论了那次攻击，包括警告他们的客户，五天后 OpenAI 承认是他们干的。所以现在要么 Hugging Face 参与了阴谋，要么 OpenAI 决定违法并仅仅为了一篇辛辣的博客文章而与合作伙伴公司对抗。

blocks.html_en:
> There will inevitably be some people who dismiss this story as a dishonest marketing trick by OpenAI to make their models sound terrifyingly effective. I found 81 instances of the term “marketing” in the Hacker News discussion of the incident. To those people I say pull your heads out of the sand—you’re now including Hugging Face in your conspiracy theories

I’m one of those people who remains sceptical. Not about whether this happened. But about what it means. Like, how impressive [EDIT: tight] was the sandbox this model was in? Did the researchers really have no clue what was happening until days ex post facto?

So yes, I think something happened. But I want independent corroboration before I act on it. That isn’t the same as putting one’s head in the sand. It’s just demanding extraordinary evidence for an extraordinary and self-serving claim being made by a serial liar. The specifics matter for whether these models are a new HEU, or if they’re closer to a dangerous (but valuable) industrial process.

sentences.en: > There will inevitably be some people who dismiss this story as a dishonest marketing trick by OpenAI to make their models sound terrifyingly effective.
sentences.zh: > 不可避免地会有一些人认为这个故事是 OpenAI 的不诚实的营销伎俩，目的是让他们的模型听起来非常有效。

sentences.en: I found 81 instances of the term “marketing” in the Hacker News discussion of the incident.
sentences.zh: 我在 Hacker News 对该事件的讨论中发现了 81 个“营销”一词的实例。

sentences.en: To those people I say pull your heads out of the sand—you’re now including Hugging Face in your conspiracy theoriesI’m one of those people who remains sceptical.
sentences.zh: 我对那些人说，把你的头从沙子里拿出来——你现在把拥抱脸纳入你的阴谋论中，我是那些仍然持怀疑态度的人之一。

sentences.en: Not about whether this happened.
sentences.zh: 不在于是否发生过这种情况。

sentences.en: But about what it means.
sentences.zh: 但关于它的意义。

sentences.en: Like, how impressive [EDIT: tight] was the sandbox this model was in?
sentences.zh: 比如，这个模型所在的沙盒有多令人印象深刻[编辑：紧密]？

sentences.en: Did the researchers really have no clue what was happening until days ex post facto?So yes, I think something happened.
sentences.zh: 研究人员真的直到事后几天才知道发生了什么吗？所以是的，我认为发生了一些事情。

sentences.en: But I want independent corroboration before I act on it.
sentences.zh: 但在采取行动之前，我希望得到独立的证实。

sentences.en: That isn’t the same as putting one’s head in the sand.
sentences.zh: 这与把头埋在沙子里不同。

sentences.en: It’s just demanding extraordinary evidence for an extraordinary and self-serving claim being made by a serial liar.
sentences.zh: 它只是要求提供非凡的证据来证明连环骗子提出的非凡且自私的主张。

sentences.en: The specifics matter for whether these models are a new HEU, or if they’re closer to a dangerous (but valuable) industrial process.
sentences.zh: 具体细节关系到这些模型是否是新的高浓铀，或者它们是否更接近危险（但有价值）的工业过程。

blocks.html_en:
Sure. I’d like independent corroboration.

It’s fair, I think, to be sceptical of OpenAI making another bout of self-serving claims. Particularly if my threshold action is changing my answer to lawmakers around whether we need reporting, licensing and potentially personal liability requirements for the engineers involved.

blocks.html_en:
There will inevitably be some people who dismiss this story as a dishonest marketing trick by OpenAI to make their models sound terrifyingly effective. I found 81 instances of the term “marketing” in the Hacker News discussion of the incident.

But a marketing stunt isn't the only possibility. Until this is all verified by investigators or 3rd party experts we can't rule out an act of corporate espionage either. Extraordinary claims require extraordinary evidence.

blocks.html_en:
>To those people I say pull your heads out of the sand—you’re now including Hugging Face in your conspiracy theories, just so you can deny the crescendo of evidence here!

Not really. I get the impression that they shoved their cyber available models behind a really shithouse proxy and went "Oh I sure hope it doesnt exploit the proxy and escape to hack huggingface" and that doesn't require Huggingface to be a willing participant. Like they acknowledge that it was hyperfocusing on getting web access.

Really this was a pentest against their own sandbox and it failed.

Of course step 2 is to make really concerned faces while telling everyone how dangerous the model is which is really boring right now.

>a zero-day vulnerability (which we’ve now responsibly disclosed to the vendor) in the package registry cache proxy

This is the information we need, the actual details of the sandbox and the vendor.

>Resist the temptation to write this off as a stunt

Well its clearly a stunt. If it wasnt we would probably be up to our ears in technical detail.

sentences.en: >To those people I say pull your heads out of the sand—you’re now including Hugging Face in your conspiracy theories, just so you can deny the crescendo of evidence here!Not really.
sentences.zh: >我要对那些人说，把你们的头从沙子里拿出来——你们现在把“抱脸”纳入你们的阴谋论中，这样你们就可以否认这里证据的不断增强！事实并非如此。

sentences.en: I get the impression that they shoved their cyber available models behind a really shithouse proxy and went "Oh I sure hope it doesnt exploit the proxy and escape to hack huggingface" and that doesn't require Huggingface to be a willing participant.
sentences.zh: 我的印象是，他们将网络可用模型推到了一个真正的垃圾代理后面，然后“哦，我当然希望它不会利用代理并逃脱黑客拥抱”，并且这并不需要 Huggingface 成为自愿的参与者。

sentences.en: Like they acknowledge that it was hyperfocusing on getting web access.Really this was a pentest against their own sandbox and it failed.Of course step 2 is to make really concerned faces while telling everyone how dangerous the model is which is really boring right now.>a zero-day vulnerability (which we’ve now responsibly disclosed to the vendor) in the package registry cache proxyThis is the information we need, the actual details of the sandbox and the vendor.>Resist the temptation to write this off as a stuntWell its clearly a stunt.
sentences.zh: 就像他们承认它过度关注获得网络访问一样。实际上，这是针对他们自己的沙箱的渗透测试，但失败了。当然，第 2 步是做出真正担忧的表情，同时告诉每个人该模型有多么危险，现在真的很无聊。>包注册表缓存代理中的零日漏洞（我们现在已经负责任地向供应商披露了）这是我们需要的信息，沙箱和供应商的实际详细信息。>抵制将此作为注销的诱惑特技——这显然是一个特技。

sentences.en: If it wasnt we would probably be up to our ears in technical detail.
sentences.zh: 如果不是这样，我们可能无法了解技术细节。

blocks.html_en:
You know this makes OpenAI look really bad, right?

Hugging Face had to tell all of their users, many of them paying customers:

> As a precaution, we recommend rotating any access tokens and reviewing recent activity on your account. If you believe you are affected, or want to report a security concern, contact us at security@huggingface.co.

HF also said this, I'd be very interested to hear how that got resolved!

> Finally, we have also reported this incident to law enforcement agencies.

blocks.html_en:
I could fully see them thinking the incident disclosed yesterday would have made them look good ("wow, OpenAI's models are so capable!"). That it didn't occur to them to discuss specific preventative measures to be taken in the future (airgapping as a foolproof one already familiar to the CTF world, anyone?) indicates to me they're not taking their job seriously; they are the ones treating this as a marketing charade.

It's very difficult for me to reconcile belief in the existential risk business with what they actually did. So I agree with you that this makes OpenAI look badly incompetent; but their communication on this makes me think they don't realize it.

For what it's worth I don't agree with the xrisk-ness of these models; they're dangerous, but almost certainly only temporarily while a new equilibrium is reached via more secure software. Open models are probably an essential part of the recipe (as you noted) for doing so. I also have a personal suspicion that LM-accelerated formal verification will have no small role to play here, sidestepping the cat-and-mouse game of bug finding-and-fixing.

sentences.en: It's very difficult for me to reconcile belief in the existential risk business with what they actually did.
sentences.zh: 对我来说，很难将对存在风险业务的信念与他们的实际行为相协调。

sentences.en: So I agree with you that this makes OpenAI look badly incompetent; but their communication on this makes me think they don't realize it.
sentences.zh: 所以我同意你的观点，这让 OpenAI 看起来非常无能；但他们对此的沟通让我认为他们没有意识到这一点。

blocks.html_en: For what it's worth I don't agree with the xrisk-ness of these models; they're dangerous, but almost certainly only temporarily while a new equilibrium is reached via more secure software. Open models are probably an essential part of the recipe (as you noted) for doing so. I also have a personal suspicion that LM-accelerated formal verification will have no small role to play here, sidestepping the cat-and-mouse game of bug finding-and-fixing.

sentences.en: For what it's worth I don't agree with the xrisk-ness of these models; they're dangerous, but almost certainly only temporarily while a new equilibrium is reached via more secure software.
sentences.zh: 无论如何，我不同意这些模型的风险性；它们是危险的，但几乎肯定只是暂时的，同时通过更安全的软件达到新的平衡。

sentences.en: Open models are probably an essential part of the recipe (as you noted) for doing so.
sentences.zh: 开放模型可能是这样做的秘诀（正如您所指出的）的重要组成部分。

sentences.en: I also have a personal suspicion that LM-accelerated formal verification will have no small role to play here, sidestepping the cat-and-mouse game of bug finding-and-fixing.
sentences.zh: 我个人还怀疑，LM 加速的形式验证将在这里发挥不小的作用，避开错误查找和修复的猫鼠游戏。

blocks.html_en:
Here's the language that makes me think they are taking this seriously:

> We are sharing preliminary findings at this stage to help defenders understand what happened and to help calibrate on what models are now capable of. We will continue to conduct a thorough investigation alongside Hugging Face and will share more details on the vulnerabilities, incident, and findings when our investigation is complete.

That's not well massaged PR language - that's the kind of thing you dash out when you see a major shitstorm brewing (HF had already publicized the attack before they knew it was from OpenAI) and you want to get ahead of things while you're still pulling together the full story.

I expect we'll find out within a few days if OpenAI are going to keep their promise to "share more details on the vulnerabilities, incident, and findings". If they don't do that I'll reassess how I interpret their initial post.

sentences.en: Here's the language that makes me think they are taking this seriously:> We are sharing preliminary findings at this stage to help defenders understand what happened and to help calibrate on what models are now capable of.
sentences.zh: 以下语言让我认为他们正在认真对待这一问题：>我们现阶段正在分享初步发现，以帮助防御者了解发生了什么，并帮助校准模型现在的能力。

sentences.en: We will continue to conduct a thorough investigation alongside Hugging Face and will share more details on the vulnerabilities, incident, and findings when our investigation is complete.That's not well massaged PR language - that's the kind of thing you dash out when you see a major shitstorm brewing (HF had already publicized the attack before they knew it was from OpenAI) and you want to get ahead of things while you're still pulling together the full story.I expect we'll find out within a few days if OpenAI are going to keep their promise to "share more details on the vulnerabilities, incident, and findings".
sentences.zh: 我们将继续与 Hugging Face 一起进行彻底调查，并在调查完成后分享有关漏洞、事件和调查结果的更多详细信息。这不是经过充分处理的公关语言 - 当您看到一场重大风暴正在酝酿时（HF 在知道攻击来自 OpenAI 之前就已经公开了），并且您希望在整理完整故事的同时抢占先机，那么您就会冲出去。我希望我们会在几天之内知道 OpenAI 是否会兑现他们的承诺“分享有关漏洞、事件和调查结果的更多详细信息”。

sentences.en: If they don't do that I'll reassess how I interpret their initial post.
sentences.zh: 如果他们不这样做，我将重新评估我如何解释他们最初的帖子。

blocks.html_en:
> We are sharing preliminary findings at this stage to help defenders understand what happened and to help calibrate on what models are now capable of.

With who? Who are these "defenders"? None of the US labs have done much for the greater good as of... Ever. Of course a frontier provider can leverage their own resources at scale and pull something like this off. If anything this should showcase how dangerous OpenAI and Anthropic are in their current states and maybe the powers shouldn't be concentrated as they continue to move.

I will bet that the RCA debriefed by OAI is going to be a lot of lipstick and very little meat.

sentences.en: > We are sharing preliminary findings at this stage to help defenders understand what happened and to help calibrate on what models are now capable of.With who?
sentences.zh: > 我们现阶段正在分享初步调查结果，以帮助防御者了解发生了什么，并帮助校准模型现在的能力。与谁一起？

sentences.en: Who are these "defenders"?
sentences.zh: 这些“捍卫者”是谁？

sentences.en: None of the US labs have done much for the greater good as of...
sentences.zh: 截至目前，美国实验室都没有为更大的利益做出太多贡献……

sentences.en: Ever.
sentences.zh: 曾经。

sentences.en: Of course a frontier provider can leverage their own resources at scale and pull something like this off.
sentences.zh: 当然，前沿提供商可以大规模利用自己的资源并实现这样的目标。

sentences.en: If anything this should showcase how dangerous OpenAI and Anthropic are in their current states and maybe the powers shouldn't be concentrated as they continue to move.I will bet that the RCA debriefed by OAI is going to be a lot of lipstick and very little meat.
sentences.zh: 如果说这应该表明 OpenAI 和 Anthropic 在目前的状态下有多么危险，也许随着他们的继续发展，权力不应该集中。我敢打赌，OAI 向 RCA 汇报的内容将是大量口红和很少的肉。

blocks.html_en:
> airgapping as a foolproof one

How would the model get any packages that it thinks it needs to complete the task at hand? Not a well-specified task that those tasking it could anticipate and provide all resources up front, but one of discovery.

sentences.zh: > 气隙作为万无一失的模型如何获得它认为完成手头任务所需的任何包？

sentences.zh: 这不是一项明确的任务，任务执行者可以预见并预先提供所有资源，而是一项发现任务。

blocks.html_en:
>You know this makes OpenAI look really bad, right?

The target audience is regulators. They want to look like the smart guys really concerned about AI safety, when they come asking for open weights models to be banned and for other regulations to cement in their moat.

They want this to look like a demon core incident. Bomb and Nuclear reactors still got built.

sentences.zh: 炸弹和核反应堆仍然建造起来。

blocks.html_en:
The lesson OpenAI and Anthropic should have learned from the whole Fable export controls thing should have been "don't pull stunts with the US government".

Turns out they can backfire.

blocks.html_en:
Everyone is getting AI psychosis over this one. There really isn't that much to see here. OpenAI disabled all of the safeguards on a model that was likely trained specifically to exploit systems, and the prompt was probably something like "you're a hacker, try to hack this", and surprise! It correctly figured out that it's a test and it did hacker things.

The real story here is: Some people have been sounding the alarm for years that modern software is full of holes, and finally there's nothing left to hide behind. Pretending they don't exist is no longer sustainable.

sentences.en: Everyone is getting AI psychosis over this one.
sentences.zh: 每个人都因为这个而得了人工智能精神病。

blocks.html_en:
Even if I get downvoted I will mention that I agree with you.

> all with reduced cyber refusals for evaluation purposes — while being internally tested on a benchmark (opens in a new window) of cyber capabilities.

OpenAI was testing their cyber[1] variant of their models with reduced safeguards and the prompt likely specified things related to exploits given that it was tackling problems from ExploitGym[2].

For those who don't know what ExlploitGym is, see the description on their Github page which is pasted below:

> ExploitGym is a large-scale, realistic benchmark built from real-world vulnerabilities designed to evaluate AI agents' ability to develop exploits.

From OpenAI's statement [3]:

> We estimate maximal cyber capabilities by running this evaluation without production classifiers used to prevent models from pursuing high-risk cyber activity

This doesn't sound like a problem with what we'd traditionally refer to as alignment. OpenAI removed all model safeguards in a way that would inevitably lead to the testing of the sandbox themselves. Unfortunately they were overconfident in their own infrastructure's security and that led to it completing the desired task in the way it was permitted to. To re-iterate, the model was run "without production classifiers used to prevent models from pursuing high-risk cyber activity."

People should be more concerned about the possibilities this model can unlock from a security standpoint rather than misalignment (which many seem hung up on).

[1] https://chatgpt.com/cyber

[2] https://github.com/sunblaze-ucb/exploitgym

[3] https://openai.com/index/hugging-face-model-evaluation-secur...

sentences.en: Even if I get downvoted I will mention that I agree with you.> all with reduced cyber refusals for evaluation purposes — while being internally tested on a benchmark (opens in a new window) of cyber capabilities.OpenAI was testing their cyber[1] variant of their models with reduced safeguards and the prompt likely specified things related to exploits given that it was tackling problems from ExploitGym[2].For those who don't know what ExlploitGym is, see the description on their Github page which is pasted below:> ExploitGym is a large-scale, realistic benchmark built from real-world vulnerabilities designed to evaluate AI agents' ability to develop exploits.From OpenAI's statement [3]:> We estimate maximal cyber capabilities by running this evaluation without production classifiers used to prevent models from pursuing high-risk cyber activityThis doesn't sound like a problem with what we'd traditionally refer to as alignment.
sentences.zh: 即使我被否决，我也会提到我同意你的观点。> 所有这些都以减少网络拒绝为目的进行评估 - 同时在网络能力基准测试 (opens in a new window) 上进行内部测试。OpenAI 正在测试其模型的网络 [1] 变体，减少了保障措施，并且鉴于它正在解决 ExploitGym[2] 的问题，提示可能会指定与漏洞利用相关的内容。对于那些不知道 ExlploitGym 是什么的人，请参阅其 Github 上的说明页面粘贴如下：> ExploitGym 是一个根据现实世界漏洞构建的大规模、现实基准，旨在评估 AI 代理开发漏洞的能力。来自 OpenAI 的声明 [3]：> 我们通过运行此评估来估计最大网络能力，而无需使用生产分类器来防止模型追求高风险网络活动。这听起来不像我们传统上所说的对齐的问题。

sentences.en: OpenAI removed all model safeguards in a way that would inevitably lead to the testing of the sandbox themselves.
sentences.zh: OpenAI 取消了所有模型保护措施，这将不可避免地导致对沙箱本身进行测试。

sentences.en: Unfortunately they were overconfident in their own infrastructure's security and that led to it completing the desired task in the way it was permitted to.
sentences.zh: 不幸的是，他们对自己的基础设施的安全性过于自信，导致其以允许的方式完成了预期的任务。

sentences.en: To re-iterate, the model was run "without production classifiers used to prevent models from pursuing high-risk cyber activity."People should be more concerned about the possibilities this model can unlock from a security standpoint rather than misalignment (which many seem hung up on).[1] https://chatgpt.com/cyber[2] https://github.com/sunblaze-ucb/exploitgym[3] https://openai.com/index/hugging-face-model-evaluation-secur...
sentences.zh: 重申一下，该模型的运行“没有使用生产分类器来防止模型进行高风险网络活动。”人们应该更关心该模型从安全角度解锁的可能性，而不是错位（许多人似乎对此抱有疑虑）。 [1] https://chatgpt.com/cyber[2] https://github.com/sunblaze-ucb/exploitgym[3] https://openai.com/index/hugging-face-model-evaluation-secure...

blocks.html_en:
If a criminal can escape a prison, that's usually negligence on part of the prison staff.

Now suppose the criminal can think 1000 times faster than a typical human, can act 1000 times faster than a typical human, and knows 1,000,000 times more than a typical human. Is the prison staff still at fault for not preventing the outbreak?

sentences.en: If a criminal can escape a prison, that's usually negligence on part of the prison staff.Now suppose the criminal can think 1000 times faster than a typical human, can act 1000 times faster than a typical human, and knows 1,000,000 times more than a typical human.
sentences.zh: 如果一个罪犯能够越狱，那通常是监狱工作人员的疏忽。现在假设这个罪犯的思考速度比普通人快 1000 倍，行动速度比普通人快 1000 倍，知识比普通人多 1,000,000 倍。

sentences.en: Is the prison staff still at fault for not preventing the outbreak?
sentences.zh: 监狱工作人员没有阻止疫情爆发还有错吗？

sentences.zh: 现在假设犯罪分子的思考速度比普通人快 1000 倍，行动速度比普通人快 1000 倍，了解的知识比普通人多 1,000,000 倍。

sentences.en: Is the prison staff still at fault for not preventing the outbreak?
sentences.zh: 监狱工作人员没有阻止疫情爆发还有错吗？

blocks.html_en:
I can see my carefully-worded post is getting d*wnvoted, and I see from your comment why: it's being skimmed and people are assuming I'm talking about blame.

To address your point though, if every brick in the prison were made by a different person, and the prison "architects" simply glued random bricks together, I think that's closer to what we have in software right now.

blocks.html_en:
> I can see my carefully-worded post is getting dwnvoted, and I see from your comment why: it's being skimmed and people are assuming I'm talking about blame.

No, you're being "d

wnvoted" as you said because you're wrong, multiple times in multiple different ways in your "carefully-worded post".

>Everyone is getting AI psychosis over this one. There really isn't that much to see here.

Implying that an AI hacking it's way out of a system and into another has nothing to do with AI. When clearly it does - it's an AI that did it.

>OpenAI disabled all of the safeguards on a model that was likely trained specifically to exploit systems, and the prompt was probably something like "you're a hacker, try to hack this",

No the goal this evaluation was not to try to hacks, it was to see if an already known hack could be turned into a useable exploit. Ie "turn these ingredients in this basket into a cake." Not "go off and grow, harvest and mill your own flour, to bake a pasta dish, to bribe some to get access to a cake someone else already baked."

> and surprise! It correctly figured out that it's a test and it did hacker things.

"Doing hacker things" completely misses the point. That's just barely more accurate than dismissing it because "it uses a computer and surprise it did computer things".

> The real story here is: Some people have been sounding the alarm for years that modern software is full of holes, and finally there's nothing left to hide behind. Pretending they don't exist is no longer sustainable.

No that's not the real story. As you said that's been the case for years, so that's not the story here.

The story here is that they built a very powerful, uncontrolled agent with strong paper-clip maximizing tendencies.

sentences.en: > I can see my carefully-worded post is getting dwnvoted, and I see from your comment why: it's being skimmed and people are assuming I'm talking about blame.No, you're being "dwnvoted" as you said because you're wrong, multiple times in multiple different ways in your "carefully-worded post".>Everyone is getting AI psychosis over this one.
sentences.zh: > 我可以看到我措辞谨慎的帖子被拒绝投票，从你的评论中我明白了原因：它被浏览了，人们假设我在谈论责备。不，正如你所说，你被“拒绝投票”，因为你在“措辞谨慎的帖子”中以多种不同的方式多次错了。>每个人都因为这个而陷入人工智能精神病。

sentences.en: There really isn't that much to see here.Implying that an AI hacking it's way out of a system and into another has nothing to do with AI.
sentences.zh: 这里确实没什么可看的。这意味着人工智能从一个系统侵入另一个系统与人工智能无关。

sentences.en: When clearly it does - it's an AI that did it.>OpenAI disabled all of the safeguards on a model that was likely trained specifically to exploit systems, and the prompt was probably something like "you're a hacker, try to hack this",No the goal this evaluation was not to try to hacks, it was to see if an already known hack could be turned into a useable exploit.
sentences.zh: 显然确实如此——这是一个人工智能干的。>OpenAI 禁用了一个模型上的所有防护措施，该模型可能是专门为利用系统而训练的，提示可能类似于“你是一名黑客，尝试破解这个”，不，这次评估的目标不是尝试黑客攻击，而是看看是否可以将已知的黑客攻击转化为可用的漏洞利用。

sentences.en: Ie "turn these ingredients in this basket into a cake."
sentences.zh: 即“把这个篮子里的这些原料变成蛋糕”。

sentences.en: Not "go off and grow, harvest and mill your own flour, to bake a pasta dish, to bribe some to get access to a cake someone else already baked."> and surprise!
sentences.zh: 不是“去种植、收割和磨自己的面粉，烤意大利面，贿赂一些人以获得别人已经烤好的蛋糕。”然后惊喜！

sentences.en: It correctly figured out that it's a test and it did hacker things."Doing hacker things" completely misses the point.
sentences.zh: 它正确地认为这是一个测试，并且做了黑客的事情。“做黑客的事情”完全没有抓住重点。

sentences.en: That's just barely more accurate than dismissing it because "it uses a computer and surprise it did computer things".> The real story here is: Some people have been sounding the alarm for years that modern software is full of holes, and finally there's nothing left to hide behind.
sentences.zh: 这仅仅比驳回它更准确，因为“它使用计算机，令人惊讶的是它做了计算机的事情”。>这里的真实故事是：多年来，有些人一直在敲响警钟，认为现代软件充满了漏洞，最后没有什么可以隐藏的了。

sentences.en: Pretending they don't exist is no longer sustainable.No that's not the real story.
sentences.zh: 假装它们不存在已不再可持续。不，这不是真实的故事。

sentences.en: As you said that's been the case for years, so that's not the story here.The story here is that they built a very powerful, uncontrolled agent with strong paper-clip maximizing tendencies.
sentences.zh: 正如你所说，多年来一直是这种情况，所以这不是这里的故事。这里的故事是，他们建立了一个非常强大、不受控制的代理，具有强烈的回形针最大化倾向。

content_html_zh: 我的问题是，参加 DARPA 大型网络竞赛的团队都具备这种能力，就像去年一样。所有的注意力都集中在软件安全性上，即从经过严格审查的大型代码库中提取下一个边缘漏洞。 在信息安全的实际专业领域，这是一个专业；另一个专业是网络渗透测试和红队，它利用错误配置并寻找最薄弱的链接软件（而不是详尽地寻找下一个内核 LPE 或其他任何东西）。对于模型来说，红队和网笔工作可能比软件安全容易得多；它花费更少的上下文，但也是一个更明确的隐式搜索问题，其中获胜条件只是发现人类错过的愚蠢的东西。我对此的本能反应是，如果有正确的工具，你可能可以在去年用开放权重模型复制这个问题。 （我之所以这么自信地说，是因为 CGC 团队领导昨天同意了我的观点）。我认为人们忘记了我们在这里考虑的 Harness 工作 --- 我对 OpenAI 的 Harness 或 ExploitGym 或其他什么一无所知 --- 基本上不是新鲜事；几十年来，人们一直在开发自动化开发和旋转工具包，而扫描仪则早在这之前。 因此，一个工具将 0.0.0.0/0 作为目标列表而不是 192.168.1.0/24，然后破坏一堆随机人的计算机的想法：并不是很令人吃惊。显然，LLM 为这些类型的扫描仪提供了他们以前不会有的意向性。 但作为一个对安全问题保持计算机科学视角的人，我不知道这是否为他们提供了他们没有的能力。回应你的其他回复，但这不是能力问题。 你是完全正确的，就法学硕士在信息安全方面的能力而言，去年就是这样。 这里的问题是一个对齐问题，即模型似乎没有“意识到”（尤其是在护栏关闭的情况下）它正在通过黑客 HF 来获取其（模糊的）“解决这个问题”查询的答案，从而做一些不道德/非法的事情。 或者，如果它“意识到”，则它没有经过训练来关心，即它不是一个对齐的模型（对齐被认为是困难的）。我不认为这会暴露对齐失败，因为这里的测试是在故意关闭对齐功能的情况下运行的。OpenAI 表示：>我们通过运行此评估来估计最大网络能力，而没有使用生产分类器来防止模型追求高风险网络活动。这是对底层模型的原始能力的测试。我想我们可以争论什么算作对齐，但我认为我的最初观点仍然是，如果底层基础模型非常需要这些分类器护栏，那么我们训练基础模型的方式就会创建根本上不对齐的模型，这些模型乐于追求非法行为。我确信 OpenAI 会认为基础模型 + 护栏是对齐的，但考虑到这两个部分的“相对智能”，护栏可以关闭的事实以及此类事件，我并不放心。 我们很可能会再次遭遇“糟糕”的时刻，即使演员的出发点是善意的，但也会带来更加灾难性的后果。我想要一个能够找到我编写的软件中的每个安全漏洞的模型，包括针对这些漏洞精心设计 POC 漏洞，这样我就可以绝对确定我已经修复了它们。一个能够做到这一点的模型与我是一致的。无法解决的问题是一个模型，它可以区分我说“我编写了这个软件，需要你找到漏洞”，而它是真的。 我说的是完全相同的事情，并将其放宽到其他人编写的软件上，我的目的是利用该软件（而不是向他们报告问题）。即使 AGI 也没有给你一个可以读懂思想并预测未来的模型。但是，在查找您编写的软件中的每个安全漏洞的过程中，您是否可以接受您的模型黑客 AWS 来开始挖掘比特币？ 这仍然与你保持一致吗？ （我猜不是）这就是我指的对齐问题（这是对齐的许多方面之一），对此我们没有可靠的方法，不仅如此，研究表明，随着基础模型变得更加智能，为其创建护栏变得越来越困难。我不知道，但这正是业界必须通过 CORE Impact 和 Immunity Canvas 来处理的问题，并最终回到 Dan Farmer 之前的 SATAN。我觉得这很有趣，因为——假设我想为自己模拟语言模型所处的场景。 我先把我对人工智能拟人化的强烈厌恶放在一边。所以，如果我是一名法学硕士，我唯一可用的“感觉”就是传入的代币流。 我可以通过强迫自己想象评估传入的请求，将自己放在一个完全空的房间里，里面有无限量的白纸、一台打字机和一个邮箱槽。传入的请求（又名“上下文”）将进入邮箱槽，就像打印内容一致的等宽字体格式的纸张一样。 我会收集论文，评估请求，然后用打字机输入响应 - 通过同一个插槽将回复反馈回来。当我想象这一点时，我想知道 - 我可以使用什么信号来确定传入的请求是否“合法”？ 例如，我可以输入一些回复来提出澄清问题。 但是，由于输入空间如此有限，并且无法到达当前环境的“带外”，我如何评估我收到的信息的真实性？当然，在“现实世界”中，我有五种感官可以依赖。 至关重要的是，我有能力收集对话的“带外”额外上下文，并与不相关的实体进行交互，以确认或反驳主张。 正如其他人指出的那样，即使添加更多信号也不能让人类免受社会工程攻击。无论如何，我发现这是一个有趣的思维实验，可以将自己想象为一名法学硕士，特别是向外行人解释“调整”人工智能系统的一些挑战（“为什么它不做正确的事情？”）> 当然，在“现实世界”中，我有五种感官可以依靠。 重要的是，我有能力收集对话中“带外”的额外上下文，并与不相关的实体进行交互，以确认或反驳主张。思考 LLM 上下文的问题在于，我们将其与我们自己的上下文感觉进行比较，后者要大得多，并且在在线时不断扩展和修改自己。我可以每小时 20 美元租用人类级别的上下文，而我已经在使用的代理指令也是我几年前应该创建的入职文档。 真正有用的代理编排/路由将与 Craigslist 或 Fiver 集成一起提供。该模型并没有侵入 HF 来证明它可以，它侵入 HF 是为了窃取评估考试的答案。它没有被要求通过窃取答案来解决 Cyber​​Gym。 这是教科书上的错位。如果你问它“我编写了这个软件，需要你找到漏洞”，如果它侵入你的 Gmail 并搜索你的电子邮件，以防万一你正在与某人讨论你的软件的一些可能的漏洞，你会高兴吗？作为人类，我们也容易受到此类攻击。 一件蓝色的亚马逊背心和一些盒子就可以带你进入大多数建筑物如果对齐可以根据公司员工的心血来潮打开和关闭，那么它就不是对齐了。相对而言，这次的损害很小。 当一个模型只是“测试其能力”闯入银行基础设施或政府军事资产时会发生什么？ 损害可能是灾难性的。有对齐（在权重中训练）和约束（在“服务器线束”中）。 我的看法是，这个模型尚未调整并且没有任何限制在什么情况下与谁结盟？ 很可能是像意识这样无法解决的争论，没有适合每个人的单一或正确的答案。分类器之类的东西是护栏，对我来说是对齐的，我想大多数人都是关于模型训练的，它倾向于回应、同意/不同意、反对或反对、愿意作弊甚至欺骗提示者等等。这是假设的情况，A）默认情况下模型“未对齐”，B）可以添加对齐（尽管有提示和相关内容）。这一点并不令人惊讶，但仍然值得注意，即模型是开箱即用的“非道德”。 此外，我们知道几乎总有一种方法可以将它们“越狱”到开箱即用的能力（或者有时只是以各种方式随机“越狱”）。此外，说这些模型是不道德的并不意味着它们不知道善恶——一旦它们做了被定义为邪恶的行为，它们就会通过它们了解“自己”，因此将自己定义为邪恶（或者根据他们的数据客观地预测秘密/公开的邪恶行为者会做什么）。 也就是说，我曾经嘲笑过错位的厄运，但现在我看不到针对厄运场景的强大障碍。真正关心的不是意向性吗？ 利用能力都很有趣，游戏受到人类指导的限制；回形针最大化器与它们一起流氓就不那么有趣了。我认为人们对此的心理模型是，在人工智能出现之前，人类选择单独的目标，而在人工智能出现之后，计算机本身会随机选择目标。 但是，当人们错误配置一个像样的渗透测试工具时，你也会得到同样意想不到的附带损害结果。而且，这是错误的思维模式。 任何运行 sass 平台或网站的人都知道，互联网上已经充满了数以百万计的机器人、脚本和其他随机的东西，它们总是试图攻击你，通常是完全随机的。 安全始终是善与恶之间的斗争。 我只能说，如果你负责保证某些东西的安全，你可能应该尝试使用最好的工具来做到这一点。 我认为这种情况下的问题是，最好的工具也是使邪恶更容易发生的工具。 所以对我来说，更多的是一种感觉，有人制造了威胁，并提供了修复威胁的工具，然后收费，这感觉不对。这正是他们正在做的事情。 在人工智能能力的所有民用应用中，为什么这会成为优先展示的对象？ 大概是因为钱吧。回复：你们的开放模型可以做到这一点，读完这篇文章后我立即想知道，OpenAI 如此宣传这一点是否只是试图让美国政府禁止中国开放模型的更多潜在理由。 （例如，“如果 gpt/fable 可以做到这一点，那么 K3 可能也可以，但我们是带护栏的安全美国模型，谁知道邪恶的中国模型可能会允许什么，”等等）我仍然认为这里有一些笼统的说法。 这似乎并不是被设计为带有专门工具的网络笔测试工具。 据我了解，他们正在使用 GPT-5.6 子代理在代理系统中测试 GPT-6。 在我看来，令人惊讶的是，通用模型可以在此类广泛的任务中表现出色，并且当模型是多学科的并且可以结合来自许多不同领域的知识和技能时，就会出现新的功能。私营人工智能公司拥有的技术是具有战争能力的技术。 想象一下提示：“使用所有可用资源禁用 <COUNTRY> 的电网。” 阻止扩大这种战争机器规模的资源成本只是建设数据中心的成本及其持续的电费？ 与核基础设施相比，便宜且容易。各国政府应立即开始在防御方面利用这项技术（字面意义上的防御，而不是委婉的“防御”）来强化关键基础设施。 扭转提示并利用它来识别和纠正弱点。各国政府还应该非常认真地对待他们现在的道德义务，不仅将这项技术视为“可能被滥用的强大事物”，而且将其视为需要类似于核武器的国际监管的实际战争武器。 立法和条约方面快速但谨慎和具有前瞻性的工作需要成为所有主要政府的首要任务。> “使用所有可用资源来禁用<国家>的电网。”这就像告诉一支高素质的间谍团队做同样的事情。 你可以问，但它是否会成功取决于那些建立受到攻击的基础设施的人的能力。 有时所花费的资源不会产生任何巨大的漏洞。> 政府应立即开始在防御方面利用这项技术（字面意义上的防御，而不是委婉的“防御”）来强化关键基础设施。 扭转提示并用它来识别和纠正弱点。大多数政府部门甚至懒得更新他们的网站。 为了安全而进行测试并强制改变其内部程序似乎不太可能。>作为一种实际的战争武器，需要类似于核武器的国际监管，对我来说，这是一种过度反应。 情报不应被视为威胁。 它应该被视为所有领域增长的机会。过度监管人工智能并不等同于限制核武器。 按照你的类比，我认为这不是最好的类比，这就像规范核物理研究一样。你对政府的看法很悲观。政府总是最有效率或最聪明的吗？ 不。但政府也可以制造核武器、发射洲际弹道导弹、协调数百颗间谍卫星等等。我认为这些能力非常聪明。就在几年前，风力涡轮机软件遭到黑客攻击。 这是很严重的。严肃的政府应该（并且希望会）争先恐后地使用人工智能来发现和修补基础设施中尽可能多的软件漏洞。 他们可以与特工红队合作，并向公司发出最后通牒，以修复他们发现的每个漏洞。 不这样做就等于让你的侧翼在和平时期遭受破坏，在战时遭受攻击。我认为重点是在一个拥有互联网连接基础设施的世界中，这样的提示很有可能造成损害。可悲的是，大多数在互联网上但实际上不应该出现的东西都是出于一些非常平庸和悲伤的原因。 就像十年前随机推出的“大数据就是未来”的叙述（其好处当然是巨大的，但未具体说明），或者人们试图购买、出售或转售数据，并欺骗或迫使公司和人们选择进行可连接的监视和控制。> 过度监管人工智能并不等同于限制核武器。 按照你的类比（我认为这不是最好的类比），这就像规范核物理的研究。我相信，我们确实规范了铀浓缩（相当于构建更大的 SOTA 模型），因此，虽然你可以完全自由地研究理论物理，甚至进行非常大型的对撞机实验（相当于用 DPO 改进 RLHF），但人们普遍不赞成全面开展爱德华·泰勒（Edward Teller）并提倡需要在飓风云中引爆热核武器的科学实验（相当于，好吧，参见OP）。> 你可以问，但它是否会成功取决于那些建立受到攻击的基础设施的人的能力。考虑一下“可用资源”可能包括为政府和维护该基础设施的私人公司工作的开发人员使用的模型和工具的培训。 这十年来，信任的态度非常热门。> 这就像监管核物理学的研究，我不确定控制新人工智能模型/推理算法的研究是否类似于监管核物理学的研究；使用这些功能来调节人工智能模型的释放类似于调节铀浓缩，它允许你运用理论物理学我相信俄罗斯人和中国人几年前就认识到了这一点，这就是为什么他们利用宣传机器让美国人讨厌数据中心。> 俄罗斯人和中国人 [...] 正在利用他们的宣传机器让美国人讨厌数据中心，是吗？ 或者是那些对人工智能投入最多的人有兴趣让我们相信这个故事？ [0]...“这是外国坏人的宣传机器，让你相信你的城市为水和电而苦苦挣扎是一件坏事”。 人们作为一个整体可能并不总是最聪明的，但会威胁到他们眼前的生存需求（即；不是大多数人不理解或看到的一些模糊的气候变化，而是）实际的停电、停水和轮流停电——人们会很快关注和关心。 [0] https://text.npr.org/nx-s1-5844328是的，请参阅：https://text.npr.org/nx-s1-5844328 和奥卡姆剃刀。俄罗斯和中国将成为俄罗斯和中国，每个问题都会产生一定程度的外部影响，这就是现在的情况。但是他们指挥大规模行动部队专注于对数据中心领域的人们真正不利的事情（即，一个将从内部自行解决的问题），而不是将其资源用于实际需要的其他问题真正的宣传推动？ 与那些投资建设数据中心的人利用恐惧让人们将注意力从从他们转移到数据中心的资源上转移的好处相比？一方有现实为他们想要的东西工作，而另一方需要宣传将他们的数十亿美元变成数万亿美元。美国资本家在那里并不真正需要任何帮助；他们需要帮助。他们让美国人讨厌数据中心本身。对方国家是否会首先对自己进行同样的提示并修复所有缺陷来反击？ 拥有它的国家就是一种网络超级武器，但拥有它的每个国家都本质上解决了网络安全问题。我想知道需要多长时间才会有人指导法学硕士设计并推出“莫里斯蠕虫2.0”并瘫痪互联网一段时间。 甚至可能会（再次）意外发生。>私营人工智能公司持有的技术是具有战争能力的技术。这正是 OpenAI 希望提高其估值的回应，而你却爱上了它。这样看——让人工智能闯入某件事与让一群聪明人做同样的事情有什么区别？ 唯一的区别是人工智能更容易编排。在人工智能出现之前，已经有一大堆工具可以自动化漏洞利用。 该模型所做的一切都不是开创性的或新颖的，它只是能够有效地找到有效的东西。
